target.title: fix(ios): stabilize chat streaming layout and session flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: iOS chat streaming could attach to a synthetic placeholder lane, churn during initial history/bootstrap, and reparse streaming content in ways that caused flicker or session drift.
- Why it matters: active conversations can show unstable streaming UI, lose the correct lane/session association, or visibly flicker while tokens arrive.
- What changed: scoped streaming to the active session key, hardened stream acceptance/initial load behavior, and stabilized layout parsing/visibility updates.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol changes, no new model behavior. This fix lives in the shared `OpenClawChatUI` layer used by both iOS and macOS. The bug (cross-run UI drift during concurrent session-key agent events) affects any client using `ChatViewModel`; macOS uses the same chat view and benefits equally, but the fix is not platform-specific — it guards shared streaming state.
- AI-assisted: yes (AI-assisted implementation, contributor-reviewed).
- Testing level: targeted shared-package tests plus iOS build validation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #40985
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: streaming state was not consistently tied to the active session key, and downstream UI/layout updates re-parsed or re-evaluated visibility in ways that amplified bootstrap churn into visible instability.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was insufficient coverage around session-scoped streaming updates plus layout stability under rapid token updates/history refresh.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): this branch supersedes #40985 by fixing the broader lane/acceptance/layout causes instead of only the earlier visible symptom.
- Why this regressed now: incremental fixes addressed isolated symptoms while the newer streaming lane introduced more state transitions that needed session-scoped and layout-stable handling.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: the issue was not isolated to one text-rendering widget; it involved session scoping, bootstrap timing, and layout parsing together.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/ChatViewModelTests.swift`
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/AssistantTextParserTests.swift`
- Scenario the test should lock in: session-scoped streaming updates must survive bootstrap/history churn and preserve stable parsed output/visibility while tokens stream.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in shared chat state/parsing logic, so package-level tests cover the core behavior without requiring a full end-to-end harness.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): targeted parser/view-model tests were added/expanded for this branch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- iOS chat streaming stays attached to the active session instead of a synthetic placeholder lane.
- Streaming text/layout behaves more stably during initial load/history refresh.
- Visible flicker/churn from repeated parse/visibility mismatches is reduced.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local dev environment
- Runtime/container: shared OpenClawKit package + iOS app build
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): iOS chat UI / streaming flow
- Relevant config (redacted): local iOS simulator build

### Steps

1. Build the shared package and iOS app from this branch.
2. Open a chat session and trigger a streaming reply.
3. Observe initial history/bootstrap plus active token streaming.

### Expected

- Streaming binds to the active session.
- Bootstrap/history churn does not destabilize the active stream.
- Layout remains visually stable while tokens arrive.

### Actual

- Matched expected in targeted tests and local build validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests`
  - `xcodegen generate`
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build`
- Edge cases checked:
  - stream acceptance during bootstrap/history load
  - parser stability under repeated streaming updates
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full repository CI matrix
  - non-iOS clients

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - All 19 review threads (P1/P2 from `chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]` + `copilot-pull-request-reviewer`) resolved in this update.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A



## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: chat-state timing bugs can reappear under bootstrapping or external-final sync edge cases.
  - Mitigation: this patch scopes state to the active session and adds targeted parser/view-model coverage around the churn paths.



## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: iOS/shared OpenClawChatUI streaming state remains scoped to the active session, load recovery remains idempotent after a failed initial bootstrap plus successful manual refresh, and streaming text/tool state does not get cleared by a later view `load()` call.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS local dev environment, Xcode 26.5 SDKs, Swift Testing 1902, OpenClawKit shared package and generated iOS Xcode project from this PR head `591a724df2531d373fc8603f2faa323fc93bc053`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml
xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawChatUI -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
ChatViewModelTests: Test run with 83 tests in 1 suite passed after 5.550 seconds.
AssistantTextParserTests: Test run with 7 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.001 seconds.
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml: Created project at /Users/eulices/projects/openclaw-pr50483/apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj
xcodebuild OpenClawChatUI generic iOS Simulator: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The recovered-load regression is covered by `successful refresh after failed load makes later load idempotent`, which passed after confirming a failed first bootstrap can recover through manual refresh and a later `load()` does not issue another history request or clear the active stream. The shared `OpenClawChatUI` target also built successfully for iOS Simulator from the generated project.
- **What was not tested**: Full `OpenClaw` app scheme build was attempted but blocked by local Xcode environment missing watchOS 26.5 for the embedded Watch app; the directly changed shared chat UI target was built instead. Full repository CI matrix was left to GitHub.

target.title: fix(webchat): slash menu clipped by overflow and missing keyboard scroll
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The webchat slash command menu (`/`) is completely invisible — typing `/` changes the input border color and arrow-key selection works, but the dropdown never appears. This is because `.agent-chat__input` uses `overflow: hidden` (for textarea scroll containment), which clips the absolutely-positioned slash menu that renders above the input via `position: absolute; bottom: 100%`.
- Additionally, when the slash menu has more items than the visible scroll area, keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/ArrowDown) does not auto-scroll the active item into view — users must manually scroll with the mouse.
- What changed: Introduced `.agent-chat__input-wrap` as a positioning parent for the slash menu, so it renders outside the `overflow: hidden` container. Added `scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest" })` after each arrow-key index change.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No modifications to slash command logic, command execution, or any non-UI code.

![slash-menu-fix](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9623f14a-9a7d-43a8-82fe-f75d5be85ba2)
- Here above demonstrates the fix.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Slash command menu now appears above the input box when typing `/` in webchat.
- Arrow-key navigation in the slash menu auto-scrolls to keep the active item visible.
- No changes to existing keyboard shortcuts (Tab to fill, Enter to select, Escape to close).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin 25.2.0)
- Runtime/container: Node.js 22+
- Integration/channel: Webchat (Control UI)

### Steps

1. Start gateway: `openclaw gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force`
2. Open `http://127.0.0.1:18789/webchat/`
3. Type `/` in the chat input

### Expected

- Slash command menu appears above the input box
- ArrowDown/ArrowUp navigates through commands with auto-scroll

### Actual (before fix)

- Input border changes color (`:focus-within` fires), arrow keys + Enter work (DOM exists), but the menu is completely invisible — clipped by `overflow: hidden`
- When menu is made visible via other means, keyboard navigation past visible items requires manual mouse scrolling

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording

Before fix — typing `/` shows no menu (only border color change):
The DOM is present and functional (keyboard selection works), but the popup is clipped to zero height by `overflow: hidden` on the parent.

After fix — slash menu visible and keyboard-scrollable:
Menu appears above input; ArrowDown past visible items auto-scrolls the list.

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios:
  - Built UI locally via `pnpm ui:build`, started gateway with `node openclaw.mjs gateway run`
  - Confirmed slash menu appears when typing `/`
  - Confirmed ArrowDown/ArrowUp auto-scrolls active item into view
  - Confirmed Tab fills command, Enter selects, Escape closes
  - Confirmed input box border-radius, textarea scroll, and backdrop-filter unaffected
  - Confirmed mobile layout selector updated (`.agent-chat__input-wrap` in `layout.mobile.css`)
- What I did **not** verify:
  - macOS/iOS native app webchat (only tested browser webchat)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the `.agent-chat__input-wrap` wrapper — move `margin`/`flex-shrink` back to `.agent-chat__input` and remove the wrapper `<div>` in `chat.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: If a CSS rule targets `.agent-chat__input` for margin/positioning, it may need updating to `.agent-chat__input-wrap`.

## Files changed

- `ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css` — Added `.agent-chat__input-wrap` class; moved `margin` and `flex-shrink` from `.agent-chat__input` to the wrapper
- `ui/src/styles/layout.mobile.css` — Updated mobile override selector from `.agent-chat__input` to `.agent-chat__input-wrap`
- `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` — Wrapped input bar in `.agent-chat__input-wrap`; moved `renderSlashMenu` outside `.agent-chat__input`; added `scrollSlashMenuActiveIntoView()` helper called after ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigation

**AI-assisted:** Built with Claude Code. Tested manually on local gateway webchat.

target.title: feat(session): add chat-channel session switching with LRU history retention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Chat channels did not support listing or switching between prior sessions under the same SessionKey, and follow-up fixes were needed to preserve switchable transcripts until LRU eviction and restore per-session usage/compaction snapshots correctly.
- Why it matters: Without in-chat session switching, users cannot navigate past conversations from Feishu/DingTalk/Telegram/Discord-style chat surfaces; incorrect transcript/archive or snapshot behavior would also make `/session back`, `/sessions`, and `/status`-adjacent state feel unreliable.
- What changed: Added `/sessions`, `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for chat channels; introduced per-SessionKey LRU `sessionHistory`; preserved switchable transcripts until history eviction; restored token/context/compaction snapshots on switch; documented matching/order/retention behavior; cleaned up reset semantics so `session.historyLimit=0` truly disables switchable history.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change SessionKey routing, does not add cross-SessionKey switching, does not add named sessions/UI picker flows, and does not change channel plugin-specific transport behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #9959
- Related #28168

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Added `/sessions` to list switchable sessions for the current chat SessionKey.
- Added `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for in-chat session switching.
- Current session is included in `/sessions`, marked as `[current]`.
- `/sessions` now shows compact single-line preview rows with session ID prefix and truncated preview text.
- Session switching now restores stored token/context/compaction snapshots for the target session.
- `session.historyLimit` now consistently supports `0` as “disable switchable session history”.

target.title: fix(zai): send GLM thinking params
target.body:
## Summary
- Update the Z.AI OpenAI-completions thinking payload to send `thinking: { type }` instead of the stale `enable_thinking` flag.
- Forward the selected reasoning effort as `reasoning_effort` for Z.AI reasoning requests.
- Add a regression test covering GLM-5.2/Z.AI request params and refresh the compat comment.

## Issue
Fixes #97772

## Changes
- Z.AI reasoning models now send `thinking: { type: "enabled" }` when a reasoning effort is selected, or `"disabled"` otherwise.
- Z.AI request payloads no longer include `enable_thinking`.
- The OpenAI completions compat type documentation now reflects the current Z.AI format.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts -t 'Z.AI thinking params|forwards simple stop sequences'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/types.ts`
- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
- Added-line secret scan: PASS


## What Problem This Solves
Issue #97772 reports that Z.AI/GLM-5.2 requests still used the old `enable_thinking` boolean, while current GLM-5.2-compatible Z.AI requests expect a `thinking: { "type": "enabled" | "disabled" }` object. The mismatch can make reasoning-enabled requests fail or silently ignore the requested thinking mode.

## Evidence
Focused local validation on the exact PR head `e7695f1127af0a695112624bf08a7cddc70b253c`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts -t 'Z.AI thinking params|forwards simple stop sequences'
✓ 1 file passed; 2 tests passed, 33 skipped

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts
✓ 1 file passed; 35 tests passed

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/types.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check && git diff --cached --check
PASS

Added-line secret scan
PASS
```

The regression test captures the outgoing OpenAI-compatible payload before any network call and asserts `thinking` is `{ type: "enabled" }`, `reasoning_effort` is forwarded as `"high"`, and the stale `enable_thinking` field is absent.

target.title: fix(browser): move ssrf-runtime-internal exports to public ssrf-runti…
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
closes #96639
Fixes the browser plugin lazy-load crash where `openclaw browser status` and any browser agent tool fail with:

```
Package subpath './plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal' is not defined by "exports"
in openclaw/package.json imported from openclaw\dist\cdp.helpers-B5T7VOSu.js
```

## Why This Change Was Made

`registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass` and `fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard` were only exported from the private `openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal` subpath. Export both functions from the public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime` path and update consumer imports.

## What Changed

9 files, +10/−15 lines. No `package.json` or config changes.

## Evidence

Environment: Linux x64, Node v22.19.0, Chromium 148.

### Before (origin/main) — confirmed broken

```text
# dist file excluded from package
$ ls dist/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal.js
FILE NOT FOUND

# Node.js exports map rejects the import
$ node -e "import('openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal')"
Error: Package subpath './plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal' is not defined by "exports"

# Two functions NOT in public ssrf-runtime
$ node --import tsx -e "
const mod = await import('openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime');
console.log('registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass:', typeof mod.registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass);
console.log('fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard:', typeof mod.fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard);
"
registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass: undefined
fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard: undefined
```

### After (fix/browser-ssrf-runtime-import) — confirmed fixed

```text
# Both functions importable from public ssrf-runtime
$ node --import tsx -e "
const mod = await import('openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime');
console.log('registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass:', typeof mod.registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass);
console.log('fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard:', typeof mod.fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard);
"
registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass: function
fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard: function

# E2E: browser plugin lazy-loads, gateway returns full status
$ pnpm build && pnpm openclaw gateway start && pnpm openclaw browser status
profile:          openclaw
enabled:          true
transport:        cdp
cdpUrl:           http://127.0.0.1:18800
detectedBrowser:  chrome
detectedPath:     /usr/bin/google-chrome-stable
EXIT: 0

# Private subpath infrastructure unchanged
$ grep "!dist/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal" package.json
"!dist/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal.js"
```

### Unit tests

| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| ollama embedding-provider (25) | 25/25 passed |
| browser cdp-proxy-bypass (28) | 27/28 (1 pre-existing env issue) |
| contract guardrail (21) | 21/21 passed |
| surface-report --check | exit 0 |

### Test conclusion

| Check | Before (origin/main) | After (fix) |
|-------|---------------------|-------------|
| Public import of both functions | `undefined` | `function` |
| `openclaw browser status` | — | exit 0, full output |
| Unit tests | — | 73/74 passed |
| Private dist exclusion | unchanged | unchanged |

## Root Cause

`registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass` (`a37ebb2d49`) and `fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard` (`fd2a9adbe6`) were placed in the private `ssrf-runtime-internal` module. Its dist artifact was excluded via `!dist/` (added by `2229122077`) and it had no `package.json` exports entry. Node.js native module resolution rejected the import at runtime.

target.title: security: create session dirs with private permissions
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: managed `agents/<id>/sessions/` directories were created with default permissions, which landed as `0755` on POSIX hosts in practice.
- Why it matters: session transcripts are sensitive state, so managed session directories should default to private permissions.
- What changed: added `ensurePrivateSessionsDir(...)` for managed session directories and reused it across setup, onboarding, agent creation, and managed session store / transcript flows.
- Scope guard: caller-supplied custom session store / transcript directories still use plain `mkdir`, while managed paths now reject symlinked final or ancestor components before permission hardening, including custom `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` roots.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Managed `agents/<id>/sessions/` directories are now created as private directories (`0700` on POSIX, best-effort equivalent on Windows). Caller-supplied custom session store / transcript directories keep their previous plain-`mkdir` behavior.

## Security Impact (required)

2. Inspect the created `sessions/` directory permissions and compare before/after behavior.

### Expected

- Managed `agents/<id>/sessions/` directories are created with private permissions (`0700` on POSIX, best-effort equivalent on Windows).
- Custom caller-supplied directories are not re-permissioned.
- Symlinked managed final or ancestor paths are rejected before hardening.

### Actual

- Before this change, managed session directories inherited default permissions and landed as `0755` in local verification.
- After this change, managed session directories are explicitly hardened, while custom caller-supplied directories keep their prior behavior.

## Evidence

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

target.title: security: scan markdown skill definitions for injection threats
target.body:
## Summary

The skill scanner (`src/security/skill-scanner.ts`) previously only scanned JavaScript/TypeScript-style source files for threats. This PR extends it to also scan Markdown `.md` files, including `SKILL.md` definitions, for injection and obfuscation patterns.

Markdown-specific rules added:
- `hidden-unicode` detects zero-width characters and text-direction overrides that can hide malicious content from visual review.
- `markdown-data-uri` detects data URIs with executable MIME types such as `text/html` and `application/javascript`.
- `markdown-download-exec` detects `curl`/`wget` download-and-execute pipelines, including common bypass forms like `sudo bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, line continuations, and quoted pipe characters inside arguments.
- `markdown-encoded-payload` detects large base64 blocks inside Markdown.
- `markdown-hex-payload` detects hex-encoded payloads inside Markdown.

Implementation notes:
- Added `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS` and `isMarkdown()`.
- Added `.md` to `SCANNABLE_EXTENSIONS`.
- Applies Markdown-specific rules only to Markdown files, while preserving code-specific scanning behavior for code files.
- Uses the raw Markdown source for Markdown source-wide rules so URLs like `https://...` are not accidentally truncated by code-comment stripping.
- Adds focused tests for Markdown findings, Markdown/code rule separation, directory scanning, and summary counts.

Why this matters: skill definitions can include rendered Markdown. Without scanning these files, hidden instructions, obfuscated payloads, or download-and-execute commands in skill Markdown can bypass the existing code scanner.

Supersedes #10705 (auto-closed by stale bot). Rebased cleanly onto current `main`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm check:changed`
- [x] Direct scanner smoke for Markdown download-and-execute bypass forms plus benign Markdown prose and table negative cases

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Markdown skill definitions such as `SKILL.md` are now scanned for hidden Unicode, executable data URIs, download-and-execute pipelines, and encoded payloads that previously fell outside the JavaScript/TypeScript scanner surface.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS at `/Users/alexalaniz/Code/openclaw-43469`, based on current `openclaw/openclaw:main` (`24bd0b212f`), with dependencies installed using `pnpm@10.33.2`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the actual scanner through `node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs` against Markdown sources containing `curl`/`wget` bypass forms, then ran `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, and `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
markdown scanner smoke passed 7 positives and 2 negatives

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  32 passed (32)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.08s

All matched files use the correct format.

Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

Observed result after fix: The actual parser-based scanner caught 7 Markdown download-and-execute bypass samples in the smoke run (`sudo bash`, `sudo -E bash`, `env FOO=1 bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, a backslash-newline before `| bash`, and a quoted pipe inside a curl header) and did not flag benign curl prose or `| curl | bash |` Markdown table cells, while the targeted scanner test file and changed-file gate completed successfully.

What was not tested: No known gaps.

target.title: fix(skills): list omitted skill names when prompt is truncated
target.body:
## Problem

When many skills are installed (e.g. 45+), the prompt truncation at `maxSkillsPromptChars` (default 30KB) silently drops skills near the end of the alphabetically-sorted list. The agent sees only a generic `⚠️ Skills truncated: included N of M` message but has **no way to discover which skills were omitted** — it cannot use them even when directly relevant to the user's request.

Real-world example: a user sends a `mp.weixin.qq.com` link, but the `wechat-reader` skill (w-prefix, sorted last) is truncated out. The agent falls back to `curl`, which fails because WeChat articles are JS-rendered.

## Solution

When skills are truncated, the note now includes the **names** of omitted skills (capped at 1KB to stay compact) and suggests `openclaw skills info <name>` or `openclaw skills list` so the agent can still discover and read them on demand.

**Before:**
```
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 40 of 45. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.
```

**After:**
```
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 40 of 45. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit. Additional installed skills not expanded here: verify-on-browser, wechat-reader, web-search-plus, wrangler, video-frames. If a listed skill name becomes relevant, inspect it with `openclaw skills info <name>` or `openclaw skills list` before deciding whether to read its SKILL.md.
```

## Changes

- `applySkillsPromptLimits()` now returns `omittedSkills` alongside the existing fields
- New `formatTruncatedSkillNames()` builds a compact name list (hard-capped at 1KB)
- New `buildSkillsTruncationNote()` assembles the enriched truncation message
- Two new test cases covering name listing for both char-based and count-based truncation

## Tests

All 34 existing + new tests pass (`npx vitest run src/agents/skills`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Skills prompt truncation should keep the emitted prompt within `maxSkillsPromptChars` and advertise omitted skill identifiers that `openclaw skills info <name>` can resolve exactly, including raw keys with characters such as `<` and backticks.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Darwin arm64, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.2, branch `fix/skills-truncation-visibility`, current HEAD `db926508c4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rendered 80 synthetic workspace skills through the real `buildWorkspaceSkillsPrompt()` code path with `maxSkillsPromptChars: 520` and raw key `skill-00<zero`raw`; then parsed the emitted `<omitted_skill_keys>` payload and resolved the first advertised key through `formatSkillInfo()` exact lookup. For comparison, the same rendering script was also run against local worktree commit `79acf3289d` before the budgeted lookup-key note.

target.title: fix(hooks): deliver internal hook replies on replyable surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `event.messages` was documented as a generic user-reply channel, but only `/new` and `/reset` actually drained those messages.
- Why it matters: `message:received` hooks could push reply text that was silently dropped, while other lifecycle hooks looked supported even though they had no safe reply target.
- What changed: added a shared internal hook reply-drain helper, kept reset/new command behavior, and now deliver `message:received` hook replies when a routable reply target exists.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not make bootstrap, preprocessed/transcribed, sent, or other lifecycle-only hooks auto-reply.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #39688
- Related #40185

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Internal hook replies pushed to `event.messages` are now delivered on `message:received` when OpenClaw has a reply-capable routing target.
- Docs now explicitly limit `event.messages` auto-delivery to reply-capable surfaces instead of implying it works for all hooks.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): internal hooks / inbound reply routing
- Relevant config (redacted): default hook + routeReply test harnesses

### Steps

1. Register an internal hook that pushes text into `event.messages`.
2. Trigger it from `/new` or `message:received`.
3. Verify the reply is routed only when a safe reply target exists.

### Expected

- Reply-capable surfaces route the accumulated hook messages.
- Lifecycle-only surfaces remain non-replying unless they gain an explicit delivery contract later.

### Actual

- Before this fix, `message:received` hook replies were silently dropped.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: `commands-core` still routes reset/new hook replies; `dispatch-from-config` now routes `message:received` hook replies on routable surfaces and skips non-routable ones.
- Edge cases checked: non-routable surfaces do not auto-send hook replies; docs/comments now match that contract.
- What you did **not** verify: live messaging-provider delivery outside the existing routeReply test harnesses.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert `fix(hooks): deliver internal hook replies on replyable surfaces`
- Files/config to restore: `src/auto-reply/reply/internal-hook-replies.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-core.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`, `docs/automation/hooks.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: duplicate or unexpected hook replies on inbound turns, or `message:received` hook replies not routing when they should.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `message:received` hook replies use the same route-reply path as other provider-routed responses, so a bad target selection would misroute the hook message.
  - Mitigation: the helper uses existing inbound routing fields (`OriginatingTo`, `To`, channel normalization) and adds explicit tests for routable and non-routable surfaces.
- Risk: this branch also carries a whitespace-only `CONTRIBUTING.md` formatter cleanup required for `pnpm check`.
  - Mitigation: it is isolated in its own commit and does not affect runtime behavior.

target.title: fix(openrouter): bound OAuth API key response read in readResponseBody to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `readResponseBody` function in the OpenRouter OAuth flow uses `await response.text()` on the success path — the first body read from the OpenRouter API key endpoint. This is unbounded and can cause OOM on oversized responses. The error path already uses `readResponseTextLimited` with an 8 KB cap, but the success path was unprotected.

This replaces the success path `response.text()` with `readResponseWithLimit` (16 MiB cap), matching the codebase convention.

## Evidence

**Environment**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main @ 0ce10d7793

**Terminal output** — all 9 tests pass, including 1 oversized-response regression test:

```
$ pnpm test extensions/openrouter/oauth.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

 ✓ OpenRouter OAuth > builds the documented PKCE authorize URL 22ms
 ✓ OpenRouter OAuth > parses state-bound OpenRouter redirect URLs and query strings 5ms
 ✓ OpenRouter OAuth > exchanges an authorization code for the issued OpenRouter API key 24ms
 ✓ OpenRouter OAuth > surfaces OpenRouter OAuth exchange errors without credential material 3ms
 ✓ OpenRouter OAuth > bounds OpenRouter OAuth exchange error bodies without requiring response.text() 3ms
 ✓ OpenRouter OAuth > stores a browser OAuth result as the default OpenRouter API-key profile 9ms
 ✓ OpenRouter OAuth > uses the local callback path before opening the browser locally 3ms
 ✓ OpenRouter OAuth > exposes stable auth choice metadata 1ms
 ✓ OpenRouter OAuth > rejects oversized OpenRouter OAuth API key responses 42ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
```

The oversized test provides a `fetchImpl` mock returning an 18 MiB ReadableStream (cap is 16 MiB) and verifies the bounded reader cancels the stream and the error propagates through `exchangeOpenRouterOAuthCode` with `"OpenRouter OAuth key response exceeds"`.

## Risk

Low. The 16 MiB cap matches the codebase convention. The bounded read replaces the exact same `response.text()` call on the success path; the error path already uses `readResponseTextLimited` and is unchanged.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: Fix dashboard chat run recovery across reconnects
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes two closely related Gateway Dashboard chat reliability problems:

1. Dashboard chat could lose busy-state on reconnect/tab return and incorrectly fall back to `New session` / `Send` while the session still had an active run.
2. Gateway restarts could interrupt an in-flight chat run without leaving a visible assistant-side notice in the chat transcript.

## What changed

- include `activeChatRuns` in the gateway hello snapshot
- recover `chatRunId` / busy state from snapshot on reconnect instead of blindly clearing it
- keep the composer in busy mode while a run is still abortable
- stop forcing sidebar chat navigation back to `main`; prefer the last active chat session
- abort active chat runs during gateway shutdown and inject a durable assistant transcript notice
- process restart sentinel wake immediately on startup and inject a transcript notice for internal sessions without external delivery routes

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=6144 pnpm -s tsc --noEmit --pretty false`

## Related

- Closes #41064
- Related: #38836
- Related: #21641
- Related: #26986

commands.author: Pick-cat

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): bound bot preflight error reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`probeNextcloudTalkBotResponseFeature` reads the bot-admin error body with an unbounded `await response.text()` when the Nextcloud Talk bot-admin API returns a non-OK status during channel startup preflight. A Nextcloud endpoint that returns a large body with no `Content-Length` (broken reverse proxy, error page, or hostile/compromised host the operator pointed at) makes the gateway buffer the **entire** payload into memory before building the diagnostic — a memory-pressure vector on the startup-check path.

Affected surface: `extensions/nextcloud-talk/src/bot-preflight.ts` — the `!response.ok` branch of the bot-response feature probe.

## Why This Change Was Made

Successful JSON reads in the same probe are already bounded via `readProviderJsonResponse`, but the error branch still called `response.text()` directly. This routes that read through the existing shared bounded reader `readResponseTextLimited` (from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`) with an 8 KiB cap (`BOT_PREFLIGHT_ERROR_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES`), matching the bound already used in `send.ts`. The reader stops pulling and cancels the upstream stream once the diagnostic budget is full, so the surfaced failure message stays useful while the body can no longer grow without bound. The existing `.catch(() => "")` is preserved, so diagnostics degrade exactly as before on read failure.

No new abstraction — reuses the existing plugin-SDK helper. Plugin-local change, one call site.

## User Impact

Operators still get the same bot-preflight failure diagnostic (status + bounded body snippet) when the Nextcloud API errors during startup, but a misbehaving or hostile Nextcloud endpoint can no longer drive the gateway to buffer an arbitrarily large error body into memory.

## Evidence

**1. Real-behavior proof with a negative control (real HTTP, not mock-only).** A harness drives the **real exported** `probeNextcloudTalkBotResponseFeature` through the **real `fetchWithSsrFGuard` SSRF-guarded path** (`allowPrivateNetwork: true` to permit localhost) against a real `node:http` server that streams a **25 MiB** `503` bot-admin error body in chunks with **no `Content-Length`**, and measures bytes actually flushed on the wire before the socket closes. The negative control runs the pre-fix `await response.text()` against the same server. All endpoints are `127.0.0.1`; no secrets or private hosts involved.

```
$ tsx nc-proof.mts
Hostile bot-admin error body = 25.00 MiB, 503, streamed with NO Content-Length

[WITH FIX] probeNextcloudTalkBotResponseFeature
PASS: returns api_error — code=api_error status=503
PASS: error snippet bounded — message = 8248 bytes
PASS: upstream stopped early (cap load-bearing) — server flushed only 0.22 MiB before socket closed

[NEGATIVE CONTROL] old `await response.text()` on same server
PASS: old path buffers entire body — response.text() returned 25.01 MiB (server sent 25.01 MiB)

ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED
```

Without the fix the error read returns the full **25.01 MiB** body; with the fix the surfaced diagnostic is capped (8 KiB body + the fixed `... probe failed (503): ` prefix = 8248 bytes) and the server flushes only **0.22 MiB** before the client cancels the stream and the socket closes — proving the cap is load-bearing, not incidental. The residual ~0.22 MiB is in-flight TCP/socket buffering, not buffered by the reader.

**2. Committed regression test** (`extensions/nextcloud-talk/src/bot-preflight.test.ts`) streams an oversized error body through a tracked `ReadableStream`, asserts `response.text()` is never called, and asserts the stream is canceled after the bounded read.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/nextcloud-talk/src/bot-preflight.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
```

**3. Lint on the changed file:**

```
$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/nextcloud-talk/src/bot-preflight.ts   # exit 0
```

**What was NOT tested:** I did not point the probe at a live production Nextcloud instance and did not drive the gateway to an actual OOM. The cap is proven by bytes-on-wire + early socket close against a local server reproducing the unbounded-stream condition over the real SSRF-guarded fetch path, plus the negative control showing the old path buffering the full body.

AI-assisted: implementation, tests, and the proof harness were drafted with agent assistance; all commands above were run locally.

The PR changes Gateway browser-origin allowlist normalization so exact `chrome-extension://<id>` origins can match `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins`, with unit and WebSocket handshake coverage.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +41. Total +62 across 3 files.

target.title: Gateway: allow extension origins in browser allowlist
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` could not explicitly allowlist `chrome-extension://` because the origin checker normalized extension origins through `URL.origin`, which becomes `"null"` for that scheme. - Why it matters: extension sidepanel clients had to rely on `["*"]` to get through browser-origin WS checks, which weakened the intended hardening. - What changed: the origin matcher now keeps exact URL parsing/validation but falls back to exact `scheme://host` matching for non-standard origins, so `chrome-extension://` can be allowlisted exactly; focused unit and handshake tests were added. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): no wildcard/prefix expansion, no acceptance of literal `Origin: null`, no changes to token/device auth, host-header fallback, or non-browser client auth behavior. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [x] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #35035 (issue mentions app://* origin, but it's the same underlying problem) - Related #35396, #35057 (both PRS were fixes for this problem but were closed because the authors were banned AFAIK) ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` can now contain exact extension origins such as `chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop`, and those browser-origin Gateway WS connects will succeed when otherwise authenticated. - Operators no longer need `["*"]` solely to let a Chrome extension sidepanel client connect. - Existing exact matches for normal `http(s)` origins, wildcard behavior, host-header fallback, and literal `Origin: null` rejection remain unchanged. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` (strictly speaking this allows chrome extensions/electron+tauri apps to connect but I think calling it a new capability is a stretch) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `N/A` ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local Bun + Vitest - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WS browser-origin client; Chrome extension origin emulated in handshake tests via `ws` - Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.auth.mode="token"`, `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins=["chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop"]` ### Steps 1. Configure the Gateway with token auth and `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins=["chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop"]`. 2. Open a WS connection that sends `Origin: chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop` and a valid `connect` request. 3. Observe the handshake result. ### Expected - An exactly allowlisted extension origin should be accepted, just like an exactly allowlisted `https://...` origin. ### Actual - Before this fix, the request was rejected with `origin not allowed` because the checker collapsed the extension origin to `"null"` internally. - After this fix, the handshake succeeds and returns `hello-ok`. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: whitelisted the chrome extension I'm developing and successfully connected to the Gateway via WS - Edge cases checked: N/A - What you did **not** verify: did not try with other protocols (`app://`, `tauri://`) ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` - If yes, exact upgrade steps: `N/A` ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this commit; if extension access is urgently blocked, temporarily fall back to `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins=["*"]` until the regression is understood. - Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/origin-check.ts`; optionally restore prior `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` values in config. - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: an exactly allowlisted `chrome-extension://` still being rejected; malformed/non-matching non-standard origins unexpectedly being accepted; regressions in existing `http(s)` origin allowlisting.

target.title: doctor(config): auto-prune removed google-antigravity-auth entries
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: users can keep stale `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` config after the plugin was removed, which keeps emitting warnings (`#27371`).
- Why it matters: users must manually edit config after updates; startup/doctor warnings stay noisy until they do.
- What changed: doctor compatibility normalization now prunes stale removed plugin references from `plugins.entries`, `plugins.installs`, `plugins.allow`, `plugins.load.paths`, and `plugins.slots.memory`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider runtime/auth behavior changes; this only cleans stale config references.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #27371
- Related #13141

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw doctor --fix` now automatically removes stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin config references instead of requiring manual cleanup.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` present

### Steps

1. Add stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin references under `plugins.*`.
2. Run `openclaw doctor --fix`.
3. Re-check config and doctor output.

### Expected

- Stale removed plugin references are pruned automatically.

### Actual

- Confirmed via migration test coverage.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: ran `pnpm test src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts` and verified new migration test passes.
- Edge cases checked: removal covers entries/installs/allow/load path/memory slot in one pass.
- What you did **not** verify: full end-to-end doctor run on a live upgraded host.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unintended plugin config pruning outside removed plugin IDs.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: accidental over-pruning of plugin config if removal targeting expands incorrectly.
  - Mitigation: migration is hard-scoped to `google-antigravity-auth` and covered by targeted tests.

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where agent/provider replay could receive literal placeholders such as `(see attached image)` or empty-output fallbacks instead of normal text/JSON tool results when a tool result block was structured but not typed as `text`.

This replacement PR covers the provider conversion paths from #96895 and the active OpenAI Responses transport path noted in review.

Closes #96857
Related: #96895

## Why This Change Was Made

Several replay paths only collected tool-result blocks with `type: "text"`. Structured non-media blocks could therefore be treated as empty output and collapse to an image/no-output fallback. This PR routes affected replay paths through one shared structured text extractor so text-like structured tool output reaches provider/model context as readable text.

Covered replay paths:

- OpenAI Responses shared conversion
- OpenAI-compatible chat completions conversion
- OpenAI Responses managed transport replay
- Google/Gemini conversion
- Anthropic tool-result replay
- Mistral tool-result replay

Current fallback behavior is preserved:

- empty non-image output remains `(no output)` / provider-equivalent no-output text where that fallback already exists;
- image-only output remains an image placeholder or image input part according to the provider path;
- media-only `image`, `image_url`, `input_image`, and `audio` blocks are not serialized into provider-bound text;
- structured non-media output is serialized as JSON text instead of being dropped by text-only filtering.

## Review Updates In Current Head

Current head: `81bbe428b7ef91907030d52e7326fbb6d0beb870`.

This update addresses the latest review findings by:

- rebasing the branch onto current `main` and preserving the empty non-image no-output fallback;
- wiring the structured extractor through Anthropic and Mistral replay instead of leaving them as text-only siblings;
- keeping explicit `text` tool-result blocks text-first, so structured serialization is only used as a no-text fallback;
- capping structured fallback replay text at 8,000 UTF-16 characters before it can cross the external provider boundary while preserving existing explicit-text replay behavior;
- reusing OpenClaw's shared structured secret redaction contract via `redactSecrets()` before provider replay;
- skipping media-only block types: `image`, `image_url`, `input_image`, and `audio`;
- omitting MIME-tagged binary `data` before provider/model replay, including `mime_type`, `mimeType`, `media_type`, `mediaType`, `content_type`, and `contentType` aliases;
- redacting actual inline data URIs while preserving ordinary prose such as `metadata:ready` and `data: is ordinary prose`;
- omitting opaque/binary fields such as `encrypted_content`, `encrypted_stdout`, `blob`, `buffer`, and `bytes`;
- adding focused helper/provider regression coverage.

## User Impact

Agents can inspect structured text-like tool output in model context instead of losing it to an attachment placeholder. Existing text output and media handling remain intact, and sensitive/binary/opaque fields are redacted or omitted before external provider replay.

## Evidence

Current-head evidence is tied to `81bbe428b7ef91907030d52e7326fbb6d0beb870`.

- Live issue evidence: #96857 includes current redacted observations where ordinary text/status tool outputs reached agent-visible context as literal `(see attached image)` placeholders instead of text.
- Code-path evidence: current `main` filtered tool-result content down to `type: "text"` in the affected provider/transport paths; this patch routes those paths through a shared structured-text extractor.
- GitHub Actions `Real behavior proof` passed for this head:
  - https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379436171/job/84077730201
  - https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379481457/job/84077892770
  - https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379522284/job/84078045029
- Provider scope evidence in this branch:
  - `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` covers structured tool results and current empty/image fallback behavior.
  - `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` covers structured Responses replay plus no-output/image fallback behavior.
  - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` covers managed OpenAI Responses transport replay for structured tool output.
  - `src/llm/providers/google-shared.convert.test.ts` covers Google/Gemini function-response structured replay.
  - `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts` covers Anthropic structured non-image replay and structured text alongside images.
  - `src/llm/providers/mistral.test.ts` covers Mistral structured non-image replay and structured-only fallback behavior.
  - `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts` covers shared redaction, media-only skip, MIME-tagged binary omission, inline data URI redaction, opaque/binary field omission, uncapped text-first replay, and bounded structured fallback payloads.

Redacted provider-replay proof summary:

```text
OpenAI Responses: structured `json` tool result becomes function_call_output text containing `price`/`42`, not `(see attached image)`.
OpenAI completions: structured tool result becomes tool message text, while empty non-image output remains `(no output)` and image-bearing output remains image-specific handling.
Google/Gemini: structured tool result is serialized into function response text instead of being dropped by text-only filtering.
Anthropic: structured non-image tool_result content is serialized as JSON text; structured text can accompany image tool results without serializing media-only blocks as JSON text.
Mistral: structured non-image and structured-only tool results replay as text blocks instead of `(no tool output)`.
Shared extractor: explicit text blocks remain text-first and are not capped by the structured fallback limit; structured blocks are serialized only as a no-text fallback, capped before provider replay, redacted via `redactSecrets()`, media-only blocks are skipped, binary/opaque fields are omitted, and inline data URI payloads are replaced with length-only placeholders.
```


### Current-head redacted provider replay artifact

Source: current PR head `81bbe428b7ef91907030d52e7326fbb6d0beb870` using the shared structured tool-result replay extractor before provider conversion. The harness feeds a structured JSON tool result with secret-like fields through the current replay path and records the redacted provider-bound wire shapes.

Redacted replay text excerpt:

```text
{"type":"json","payload":{"answer":"structured-visible-text-current-head","details":{"providerReplay":true,"count":42},"apiKey":"sk-liv…leak","phone":"+15555550123"}}
```

Provider-bound replay excerpt proving structured text reaches provider/model context instead of a placeholder:

```json
{
  "openaiResponses": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_structured",
    "output": "{\"type\":\"json\",\"payload\":{\"answer\":\"structured-visible-text-current-head\",\"details\":{\"providerReplay\":true,\"count\":42},\"apiKey\":\"sk-liv\u2026leak\",\"phone\":\"+15555550123\"}}"
  },
  "anthropic": {
    "type": "tool_result",
    "tool_use_id": "call_structured",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "text",
        "text": "{\"type\":\"json\",\"payload\":{\"answer\":\"structured-visible-text-current-head\",\"details\":{\"providerReplay\":true,\"count\":42},\"apiKey\":\"sk-liv\u2026leak\",\"phone\":\"+15555550123\"}}"
      }
    ]
  },
  "mistral": {
    "role": "tool",
    "tool_call_id": "call_structured",
    "content": "{\"type\":\"json\",\"payload\":{\"answer\":\"structured-visible-text-current-head\",\"details\":{\"providerReplay\":true,\"count\":42},\"apiKey\":\"sk-liv\u2026leak\",\"phone\":\"+15555550123\"}}"
  }
}
```

Artifact assertions:

- Contains `structured-visible-text-current-head`: PASS
- Does not contain `(see attached image)`: PASS
- Secret-like input value is redacted before provider replay: PASS
- OpenAI Responses, Anthropic, and Mistral wire-shape excerpts all carry the structured text: PASS

Focused current-head regression check:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/mistral.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot --testNamePattern 'structured non-image|structured-only|structured blocks'
```

Result: PASS on current head for the focused Anthropic/Mistral structured replay coverage.

### Current-head terminal provider replay proof (redacted)

A temporary local terminal harness imported the current-head `convertResponsesMessages()` OpenAI Responses replay converter and shared `extractToolResultText()` helper from `81bbe428b7ef91907030d52e7326fbb6d0beb870`. It did not call a live external provider API; it exercised the provider-bound replay conversion boundary that builds the `function_call_output` payload sent to OpenAI Responses.

Command summary:

```text
bun .openclaw-proof-97742.mjs
```

Redacted output:

```text
PR head: 81bbe428b7ef91907030d52e7326fbb6d0beb870

[openai-responses-structured-json-only] helper_length=49
helper_text: {"type":"json","json":{"price":42,"status":"ok"}}
responses_replay: [{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_redacted","output":"{\"type\":\"json\",\"json\":{\"price\":42,\"status\":\"ok\"}}"}]
contains image placeholder: false

[openai-responses-media-plus-structured] helper_length=78
helper_text: {"type":"json","json":{"tool":"telegram","result":"readable structured text"}}
responses_replay: [{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_redacted","output":"{\"type\":\"json\",\"json\":{\"tool\":\"telegram\",\"result\":\"readable structured text\"}}"}]
contains image placeholder: false

[shared-helper-explicit-text-plus-large-structured] helper_length=46
helper_text: visible explicit text stays first and uncapped
contains image placeholder: false

[shared-helper-secret-and-binary-redaction] helper_length=237
helper_text: {"type":"json","json":{"note":"data: is ordinary prose","url":"[inline data URI: 21 chars]","token":"sk-red…oken","file":{"mimeType":"image/png","data":"[binary data omitted: 4 chars]"},"encrypted_content":"[omitted encrypted_content]"}}
contains image placeholder: false
```

This terminal proof shows structured JSON reaches OpenAI Responses replay as `function_call_output.output` text on the current head, not as `(see attached image)`. It also shows media-only blocks are not serialized into text, explicit text remains text-first, inline data URIs are length-only redacted, MIME-tagged binary `data` is omitted, and opaque encrypted content is omitted before provider replay.

## Validation

Validation performed for the current head:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot
```

Result:

```text
Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 7 passed (7)
```

Current-head GitHub Actions status checked through the PR check rollup:

- `Real behavior proof`: passed (links above)
- `check-lint`: passed — https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379437476/job/84077865192
- `check-prod-types`: passed — https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379437476/job/84077865247
- `check-test-types`: passed — https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379437476/job/84077865221
- `check-guards`: passed — https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379437476/job/84077865048

## Risk

- Message-delivery behavior changes are intentionally limited to structured non-media tool-result blocks that were previously dropped by text-only filtering.
- Explicit text tool-result blocks stay text-first and keep the existing provider replay behavior.
- Structured fallback replay text is capped before leaving the OpenClaw provider conversion boundary.
- Media-only blocks remain excluded from text extraction, so image/audio payloads keep using existing media handling paths instead of becoming provider-bound JSON text.
- Shared structured redaction runs before provider replay, and binary/opaque fields are omitted before serialization.
- Existing context/tool-result budgeting and truncation guards remain responsible for payload sizing outside the new structured-fallback cap.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous per-provider text-only extraction behavior. No migration, dependency, workflow, or persistent data changes are included.

## Not Tested / Limitations

- No live external provider API request was made by this update.
- Runtime proof is based on redacted provider-payload replay/converter paths and CI-visible checks, not on private credentials or non-public endpoints.

target.title: fix(memory): stop chunkMarkdown injecting newlines into single-line chunks
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`chunkMarkdown` (the chunker behind memory/embedding indexing) corrupts chunk text for any source that contains a single physical line longer than the chunk budget — minified JSON, base64 blobs, a long URL, a pasted log line, or flattened session text.

A long line is coarse/fine-split into fragments that all carry the **same** `lineNo`. When overlap is enabled — and it is by default (`DEFAULT_CHUNK_TOKENS=400`, `DEFAULT_CHUNK_OVERLAP=80`, `src/agents/memory-search.ts:116-117`) — the overlap carry packs several of those fragments into one chunk. `flush()` then joined every entry in `current` with `"\n"`:

```ts
const text = current.map((entry) => entry.line).join("\n");
```

That re-inserts a newline the source never had. The chunk text is no longer a substring of the file, and that corrupted text is what gets hashed, embedded (`manager-embedding-ops.ts:284`), and surfaced verbatim in search snippets (`manager-embedding-ops.ts:777,795`).

## Why This Change Was Made

`lineNo` already distinguishes fragments of one physical line from genuinely separate lines. `flush()` now joins consecutive same-`lineNo` fragments with `""` (reconstructing the original contiguous text) and emits `"\n"` only across distinct source lines. Distinct-line chunks are unchanged.

## User Impact

Memory/embedding search no longer indexes or returns text with spurious newlines spliced into long single-line content; chunk text is once again a faithful substring of the source. No config or API change.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `chunkMarkdown` injected `"\n"` (absent from the source) into chunk text when a single line exceeds the chunk budget and overlap is on (the production default), corrupting embedded text and search snippets.

**Real environment tested:** Local checkout, Node v22.22.1, the package's real `chunkMarkdown` export driven via `tsx`, plus the package Vitest suite.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
- `npx tsx` probe calling `chunkMarkdown('The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. '.repeat(89) /* 4005 chars, 0 newlines */, { tokens: 400, overlap: 80 })`, asserting every `chunk.text` is a substring of the source and contains no `"\n"`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.test.ts`
- Fail-before: restored `internal.ts` from `origin/main`, kept the new test, re-ran the filtered test.

**Evidence after fix:**
```
source has newline: false | len: 4005
chunks: 3
chunk[0] len=1600 hasNewline=false isSubstringOfSource=true
chunk[1] len=3200 hasNewline=false isSubstringOfSource=true
chunk[2] len=2405 hasNewline=false isSubstringOfSource=true
```
Distinct-line content still keeps its separators (verified: a long line + a trailing distinct line keeps the `"\n"` only between the two real lines).

Full suite:
```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  10 passed (10)
```

**Observed result after fix:** Every chunk is a substring of the source with no injected newline; all 10 `internal.test.ts` tests pass; oxfmt `--check` and oxlint clean.

**Fail-before (proves the test catches the regression):** with `origin/main`'s `flush()` and the new test, the test fails exactly at the injected-newline assertion:
```
 ❯ packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.test.ts:250:30
    250|       expect(chunk.text).not.toContain("\n");
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
```

**What was not tested:** No live end-to-end embedding/index run against a real provider (the corruption is in the pure chunker upstream of embedding, exercised directly above). This change does not address a separate, downstream-mitigated issue where the overlap carry can also make a chunk exceed the token budget (`enforceEmbeddingMaxInputTokens` hard-caps bytes downstream); that is out of scope here.

target.title: fix(launcher): stop respawned children after parent death on Unix
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #79534. When `openclaw.mjs gateway` (or any command that triggers a respawn) is run under systemd and the wrapper process is killed with SIGKILL or crashes, the spawned `node dist/index.js ...` child can survive and become an orphan reparented to PID 1. The orphan keeps ports bound, so systemd restarts fail with `EADDRINUSE` and the unit enters a crash loop.

## Why This Change Was Made

The launcher already forwards catchable signals (SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP/SIGQUIT) to respawn children, but SIGKILL and wrapper crashes cannot run that forwarding path. This change adds a parent-death guard: the wrapper passes its PID in `OPENCLAW_RESPAWN_PARENT_PID`, and the child polls `process.ppid` on Unix; if the child is reparented, it exits immediately.

The marker is consumed at guard installation so nested child commands (e.g. provider tools the respawned CLI spawns) do not inherit a stale wrapper PID.

This is a fresh implementation of the direction discussed in the closed reference PR #79571, adapted to the current launcher/entry code.

## User Impact

- Systemd-managed `openclaw gateway` units can be stopped or restarted reliably even when the wrapper is SIGKILL'd or crashes.
- No behavior change on Windows (parent-PID polling is Unix-only).
- No change for normal interactive use where the wrapper exits cleanly.

## Evidence

Local verification commands:

```text
pnpm build
pnpm test src/process/child-process-bridge.test.ts src/process/respawn-child-runner.test.ts src/process/exec.test.ts src/entry.respawn.test.ts src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 openclaw.mjs src/entry.ts src/process/child-process-bridge.ts src/process/respawn-child-runner.ts src/process/exec.test.ts test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts src/entry.respawn.test.ts src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts src/process/child-process-bridge.test.ts src/process/respawn-child-runner.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results:

- `pnpm build`: passed
- Tests: 103 passed / 2 skipped across 3 Vitest shards
- Format/whitespace checks: passed

Closes #79534

target.title: fix(chutes): bound OAuth token error response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Chutes OAuth token **exchange** and **refresh** failures read the error body with an unbounded `await response.text()`. On a hostile or misconfigured token endpoint (`https://api.chutes.ai/idp/token`) that streams a large body with no `Content-Length`, the gateway buffers the **entire** payload into memory before surfacing the error — a memory-pressure / OOM vector during auth setup and token refresh.

Affected surface: `src/agents/chutes-oauth.ts` — `exchangeChutesCodeForTokens` and `refreshChutesTokens` error paths.

## Why This Change Was Made

Successful Chutes JSON token responses are already bounded via `readProviderJsonResponse`, but the two `!response.ok` branches still called `response.text()` directly. This change routes those reads through the existing shared bounded reader `readResponseTextLimited` with an 8 KiB cap (`CHUTES_OAUTH_ERROR_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES`), matching the MiniMax OAuth error-handling pattern. The reader stops pulling and cancels the upstream stream once the diagnostic budget is full, so the surfaced error snippet stays useful while the body can no longer grow without bound.

No new abstraction — reuses the existing `readResponseTextLimited` helper. One concern, two call sites; `chutes-oauth.ts` does not touch fs-safe or any other surface.

## User Impact

Operators configuring or refreshing Chutes OAuth credentials still get the same actionable error snippet on token failures, but the gateway can no longer be driven to buffer an arbitrarily large error body into memory by a hostile or broken token endpoint.

## Evidence

**1. Real-behavior proof with a negative control.** A harness drives the **real exported** `exchangeChutesCodeForTokens` / `refreshChutesTokens` against a real `node:http` server on `127.0.0.1` that streams a **25 MiB** error body in chunks with **no `Content-Length`**, and measures bytes actually flushed on the wire before the socket closes. The negative control runs the pre-fix `await response.text()` against the same server.

```
$ tsx chutes-proof.mts
CHUTES_TOKEN_ENDPOINT = https://api.chutes.ai/idp/token
Hostile error body = 25.00 MiB, streamed with NO Content-Length

[WITH FIX] exchangeChutesCodeForTokens
PASS: error surfaced — "Chutes token exchange failed: chutes error body chutes error..."
PASS: error snippet bounded — error message = 8222 bytes
PASS: upstream stopped early (cap load-bearing) — server flushed only 0.21 MiB before socket closed

[WITH FIX] refreshChutesTokens
PASS: error surfaced — "Chutes token refresh failed: chutes error body chutes error ..."
PASS: upstream stopped early (cap load-bearing) — server flushed only 0.21 MiB before socket closed

[NEGATIVE CONTROL] old `await response.text()` on same server
PASS: old path buffers entire body — response.text() returned 25.03 MiB (server sent 25.03 MiB)

ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED
```

Without the fix the error read returns the full **25.03 MiB** body; with the fix the surfaced snippet is capped at **8222 bytes** and the server flushes only **0.21 MiB** before the client cancels the stream and the socket closes — proving the cap is load-bearing, not incidental. The residual ~0.21 MiB is in-flight TCP/socket buffering, not buffered by the reader.

**2. Committed regression tests** (`src/agents/chutes-oauth.flow.test.ts`) cover both call sites: they stream an oversized error body through a tracked `ReadableStream`, spy on `response.text()` to assert it is never called, and assert the stream is canceled after the bounded read.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/chutes-oauth.flow.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
```

**3. Lint + types on the changed file:**

```
$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/chutes-oauth.ts   # exit 0
$ pnpm tsgo                                                 # no chutes type errors
```

**What was NOT tested:** I did not hit the live Chutes endpoint and did not drive the process to an actual OOM. The cap is proven by bytes-on-wire + early socket close against a local server reproducing the unbounded-stream condition, plus the negative control showing the old path buffering the full body.

AI-assisted: implementation, tests, and the proof harness were drafted with agent assistance; all commands above were run locally.

Fixes the unbounded Chutes OAuth error-body read.

target.title: fix(agents): expose fs discovery tools to codex
target.body:
## Summary
- add ls/find/grep to the core fs tool catalog and coding tool construction
- include fs discovery tools in embedded Codex app-server construction
- update group:fs allow/deny coverage
- stabilize the slow bundle MCP tools/list timeout assertion under CI shard load

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/OpenClaw app-server coding runs that allow `group:fs` could build `read/write/edit` but not discovery tools, so workspace listing/search fell back to shell approval instead of native `ls/find/grep` tools.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the actual `createOpenClawCodingTools` runtime path. The same behavior was also hotpatched into Polytopic production while this upstream PR is pending.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx -e "import { createOpenClawCodingTools } from './src/agents/agent-tools.ts'; const names = createOpenClawCodingTools({ workspaceDir: process.cwd(), config: { tools: { allow: ['group:fs'], deny: ['exec', 'process', 'apply_patch'] } } }).map((tool) => tool.name); console.log(names.join('\n'));"`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Copied console output:
    ```text
    [agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via tools.deny: apply_patch, exec, process; matched apply_patch, exec, process
    [agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 19 tool(s) via tools.allow: agents_list, create_goal, cron, gateway, get_goal, message, nodes, session_status, sessions_history, sessions_list, sessions_send, sessions_spawn, sessions_yield, skill_workshop, subagents, tts, update_goal, web_fetch, web_search
    read
    grep
    find
    ls
    edit
    write
    ```
- Observed result after fix: `group:fs` now constructs native `grep`, `find`, and `ls` tools alongside `read/edit/write` while shell/process tools remain denied. Workspace discovery no longer requires falling back to `exec` when only fs tools are approved.
- What was not tested: Full external-provider model invocation in this PR branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Upstream merge requires an OpenClaw maintainer; Tayoun can update the fork branch and PR but cannot merge upstream. Production Polytopic verification was done via a temporary hotpatch because upstream is still pending.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, CI/test expectations and runtime construction exposed `read/write/edit` for fs policy, while `grep/find/ls` were absent from the app-server coding tool construction path.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/sandbox-explain.test.ts`
- `TMPDIR=/tmp node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts -t "keeps MCP tools/list responses"`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts`

Regression coverage updated for the coding tool catalog, sandbox `group:fs` expansion, app-server construction allowlists, and slow bundle MCP catalog timing.
