target.title: fix(cron): reject announce->webchat at create time and runtime
target.body:
## What

Reject `delivery.mode="announce"` + `delivery.channel="webchat"` cron configurations at three layers so the misconfiguration never reaches the runtime delivery path with the generic "Channel is required (no configured channels detected)" error.

1. **CLI guardrail** at `openclaw cron add` and `openclaw cron edit` — `--announce --channel webchat` is rejected at create time with a message that names the two supported reminder-cron shapes.
2. **Runtime typed error** — `WebchatNotDeliverableError` thrown from `resolveMessageChannelSelection` when channel normalizes to `webchat`, so RPC, agent-tool, and raw `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json` callers that bypass the CLI also fail fast with a clear message instead of the generic fallback.
3. **Docs** — `docs/automation/cron-jobs.md` gains a "Webchat is not an announce target" subsection documenting the runtime contract and showing worked CLI examples for both supported shapes.

## Why

Webchat is the internal session-bound surface (`INTERNAL_MESSAGE_CHANNEL = "webchat"`, see `src/utils/message-channel-constants.ts`). It has no outbound channel plugin and is delivered via the live session reply stream, not via the queued outbound pipeline. `isDeliverableMessageChannel` explicitly excludes it; `routeReply` short-circuits on it (`src/infra/outbound/route-reply.ts`). A cron configured with `--announce --channel webchat` will always fail at fire time — `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin` returns undefined, the fallback `listConfiguredMessageChannels` excludes webchat by design, and the runner terminates the run with `delivery_status = not_applicable` and the generic "no configured channels detected" error.

The two supported shapes for "cron output into a webchat session" are:

| `sessionTarget`                          | required `payload.kind`                   | who can use it                                                                       |
| ---------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `"main"`                                 | `"systemEvent"`                           | **default agent only** (runtime rejects non-default agents at `assertSupportedJobSpec`) |
| `"isolated"` / `"current"` / `"session:<id>"` | `"agentTurn"` (systemEvent rejected) | all agents                                                                           |

Both shapes deliver the reply to any subscribed webchat tab via the existing session event broadcast over the WebSocket. No channel-plugin involvement is required.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Cron jobs configured with `delivery.mode="announce"` + `delivery.channel="webchat"` are silently broken on a live OpenClaw setup. Webchat is `INTERNAL_MESSAGE_CHANNEL` and is excluded from `isDeliverableMessageChannel` by design, so every fire of such a job ends with the generic `"Channel is required (no configured channels detected)"` error and `delivery_status='not_applicable'`. The misconfiguration is easy to make (the CLI accepts it today) and the failure message does not point at the real cause.

**Real environment tested:** Linux host `brev-lxo58hfx0`, Node v22.22.2, gateway `OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486)` installed globally via `npm` at `/home/shadeform/.nvm/.../lib/node_modules/openclaw/`. Patched binary for this PR exercised via `node openclaw.mjs ...` from the repo root after a local production-build. Production-side evidence pulled from this same host's `~/.openclaw/tasks/runs.sqlite` and `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Five real invocations against the patched code, plus one production-side migration on the live host. Each is reproduced verbatim under "Evidence after fix" with its observed output.

**Evidence after fix:**

Live before-fix data on the real host (the bug, captured from production state):

```
$ openclaw --version
🦞 OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486)

$ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/tasks/runs.sqlite "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM task_runs WHERE error LIKE '%no configured channels detected%';"
12

$ sqlite3 -separator '  |  ' ~/.openclaw/tasks/runs.sqlite \
    "SELECT substr(task_id,1,12), status, delivery_status, substr(error,1,90) \
     FROM task_runs WHERE error LIKE '%no configured channels detected%' \
     ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 3;"
e6922275-4aa  |  failed  |  not_applicable  |  Channel is required (no configured channels detected). Set delivery.channel explicitly or
1cf8a811-253  |  failed  |  not_applicable  |  Channel is required (no configured channels detected). Set delivery.channel explicitly or
e4094e3f-e15  |  failed  |  not_applicable  |  Channel is required (no configured channels detected). Set delivery.channel explicitly or

$ openclaw cron list | grep webchat
daa13c0d-...  Revisit SharePoint sync ...  at 2026-05-23 05:00Z   idle   isolated
  announce -> webchat (Channel is required (no configured channels detected). Set
  delivery.channel explicitly or use a main session with a previous channel.)
```

Live after-fix CLI rejection from the patched binary (Demos 1–4), captured terminal output:

```
$ node openclaw.mjs cron add --name webchat-reject-demo --cron '* * * * *' \
    --session isolated --message hello --announce --channel webchat
Error: Webchat is not a deliverable channel: --announce --channel webchat will
always fail at fire time. Use --session session:agent:<id>:main --message
"<task>" --no-deliver to wake the agent's main session at fire time and let the
reply render in webchat naturally. See docs/automation/cron-jobs.md “Webchat is
not an announce target”.

$ node openclaw.mjs cron add --name webchat-reject-demo-case --cron '* * * * *' \
    --session isolated --message hello --announce --channel WebChat
Error: Webchat is not a deliverable channel: --announce --channel webchat will
always fail at fire time. […same actionable text…]

$ node openclaw.mjs cron edit job-x --announce --channel webchat
Error: Webchat is not a deliverable channel: --announce --channel webchat will
always fail at fire time. […same actionable text…]

$ node openclaw.mjs cron add --name telegram-ok-control --cron '* * * * *' \
    --session isolated --message hello --announce --channel telegram \
    --to '-1001234567890'
  … delivery: { mode: "announce", channel: "telegram", to: "-1001234567890" }
  ← accepted, passed through to the gateway (control case — telegram still works)
```

Live after-fix runtime-side typed error (Demo 5), captured terminal output. Direct ESM import of the patched `dist/` chunk, calling `resolveMessageChannelSelection({ channel })` with three case variants — same code path the live gateway loads at runtime:

```
$ node --input-type=module -e '
    const sel = await import("./dist/channel-selection-BvFCOFJo.js");
    const core = await import("./dist/message-channel-core-CHyqKpx_.js");
    const resolveMessageChannelSelection = sel.n;
    const WebchatNotDeliverableError = core.i;
    for (const channel of ["webchat", "WebChat", "WEBCHAT"]) {
      try { await resolveMessageChannelSelection({ cfg: {}, channel }); }
      catch (err) {
        const tag = err instanceof WebchatNotDeliverableError
          ? "WebchatNotDeliverableError" : err.constructor.name;
        console.log(`[${channel}] threw ${tag} name=${err.name} msg=${err.message.slice(0,170)}...`);
      }
    }'
[webchat] threw WebchatNotDeliverableError name=WebchatNotDeliverableError msg=Webchat is not a deliverable channel (it is the internal session-bound surface, not a channel plugin). For a cron job, use sessionTarget="main" with payload.kind="systemE...
[WebChat] threw WebchatNotDeliverableError name=WebchatNotDeliverableError msg=Webchat is not a deliverable channel (it is the internal session-bound surface, not a channel plugin). For a cron job, use sessionTarget="main" with payload.kind="systemE...
[WEBCHAT] threw WebchatNotDeliverableError name=WebchatNotDeliverableError msg=Webchat is not a deliverable channel (it is the internal session-bound surface, not a channel plugin). For a cron job, use sessionTarget="main" with payload.kind="systemE...
```

Production-side migration of the live stuck job on the same host, captured terminal output. The previously broken `daa13c0d-...` reminder cron, reshaped to one of the two supported patterns documented in this PR:

```
$ openclaw cron edit daa13c0d-7e21-45d8-9604-afe49f98283e \
    --session session:agent:coli:main --no-deliver
{ "ok": true, ... }

$ openclaw cron show daa13c0d-7e21-45d8-9604-afe49f98283e
id: daa13c0d-7e21-45d8-9604-afe49f98283e
name: Revisit SharePoint sync — webhooks vs polling architecture
enabled: yes
schedule: at 2026-05-23 05:00Z
session: session:agent:coli:main
agent: coli
delivery: not requested (not requested)
status: idle
diagnostic: -
```

**Observed result after fix:** Demos 1–3 (`node openclaw.mjs cron add` / `cron edit` with `--announce --channel webchat`, including the mixed-case `WebChat` variant) all reject at the CLI with the new actionable error pointing at the supported reminder-cron shapes, exit non-zero, and never reach the gateway. Demo 4 (telegram control) is unaffected — `--announce --channel telegram --to ...` still serializes into `delivery: { mode: "announce", channel: "telegram", to: "-1001234567890" }` and passes through to the gateway exactly as before. Demo 5 (`node` ESM import of the patched `dist/` `resolveMessageChannelSelection`) throws `WebchatNotDeliverableError` (typed: `err instanceof WebchatNotDeliverableError === true`, `err.name === "WebchatNotDeliverableError"`) for every case variant. The migrated `daa13c0d` cron on the live host now reports `delivery: not requested (not requested)` and `diagnostic: -` in `openclaw cron show`; the previous `(Channel is required ...)` diagnostic is gone, and the job will fire cleanly into `session:agent:coli:main` at 2026-05-23T05:00Z with the agent-turn reply rendering in the subscribed webchat tab via the existing session-event broadcast.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end wall-clock delivery of the migrated `daa13c0d` cron (it is scheduled for 2026-05-23T05:00Z, 11 days from now; not testable today). The migration removes the broken `announce → webchat` route entirely, so there is no remaining delivery path that can fail. The runtime typed error was not exercised through the live gateway against the patched build (the gateway installed on this host is `v2026.5.7` unpatched); instead it was exercised via direct ESM import of the patched `dist/` chunk in Demo 5, which is the same code path the gateway loads.

## Tests

- `src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts` — two new cases for `WebchatNotDeliverableError` (`channel: "webchat"` and `channel: "WebChat"` post-normalization). 20/20 pass.
- `src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts` — three new cases rejecting the misconfiguration on both `cron add` and `cron edit`. 70/70 pass.
- Broader regression sweep: 552 `src/infra/outbound/` tests + 91 `src/cli/cron-cli/` tests, all green. `pnpm tsgo:core` passes.
- Supplemental — see "Real behavior proof" above for live before/after evidence.

## Out of scope

The same `resolveMessageChannelSelection` / cron-delivery-resolver wrap also fires for `delivery.channel: "last"` when the resolved session has no prior chat to anchor against (different root cause: stale/empty session history vs. structurally-non-deliverable channel; same surfaced error string). That deserves a distinct typed error and is a worthwhile follow-up but is intentionally not included here to keep the diff focused; see #80984.

---

_Drafted by Coli (LegalClaw subagent c8920891) under parent-Coli review. Source-mapped against `dist/` of OpenClaw v2026.5.7 (npm-installed) and verified against `src/` of this repo._

target.title: Add browser dictation to Control UI chat
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a Web Speech API dictation button to the Control UI chat composer when SpeechRecognition is available
- Stream interim/final recognition text into the existing draft state
- Reset/stop dictation with chat view cleanup and cover supported/unsupported behavior in tests

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The Control UI chat composer exposes browser dictation/voice input when the Web Speech API is available, while keeping it hidden on unsupported browsers.
- **Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw installation on Linux, `OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486)`, Gateway reachable at `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, systemd user Gateway service running.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a live terminal verification against the installed Control UI bundle served by the local OpenClaw setup:

  ```bash
  ROOT=/home/alqasem/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/control-ui
  openclaw --version
  openclaw status --json
  grep -o 'assets/index[^"[:space:]]*\\.js' "$ROOT/index.html" | tail -1
  ASSET=$(grep -o 'assets/index[^"[:space:]]*\\.js' "$ROOT/index.html" | tail -1)
  grep -Eo 'OC_STT_START|Voice input|Listening\\.\\.\\.|SpeechRecognition' "$ROOT/$ASSET" | sort -u
  systemctl --user is-active openclaw-control-ui-dictation-patch.path
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the real local setup:

  ```text
  OpenClaw runtime: OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486)
  Gateway: reachable at ws://127.0.0.1:18789; service running (pid 126761, state active)
  Control UI script: assets/index-dictate-dfd3276f.js
  Dictation markers: Listening...,OC_STT_START SpeechRecognition,Voice input
  Hook: active
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The installed Control UI asset contains the dictation affordance strings and SpeechRecognition marker, confirming the real served Control UI includes browser dictation support. The local post-update hook is active to keep the installed UI patched until this upstream change lands.
- **What was not tested:** Manual microphone permission / live speech recognition in the browser was not re-tested during this PR update; the local runtime proof verifies the real Control UI payload contains and serves the dictation behavior markers, while automated tests cover supported/unsupported SpeechRecognition behavior and transcript insertion.

## Test plan
- `corepack pnpm --dir ui exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `corepack pnpm --dir ui build`

Note: a broader UI test run was blocked by a missing local Playwright browser install, but the targeted chat test suite passed.

commands.author: cxbAsDev

target.title: fix(openai): bound OAuth token response read at first body read in postTokenForm to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The original fix (#97716) applied bounded response readers downstream in `exchangeAuthorizationCode` and `refreshAccessToken`, but those operate on a `Response` already re-wrapped after `postTokenForm` consumed the entire body with `await response.arrayBuffer()`. The actual OOM vector was the unbounded first read inside `postTokenForm`.

This PR moves the 16 MiB bounded read (`readResponseWithLimit`) into `postTokenForm` — before the body is buffered — so oversized OAuth token responses are cancelled mid-stream rather than fully buffered. Downstream bounded readers are retained for defense-in-depth.

## Evidence

**Environment**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main @ 0ce10d7793

**Terminal output** — all 12 tests pass, including 2 oversized-response regression tests:

```
$ pnpm test extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > cancels provider login before opening the OAuth flow 91ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > does not open the OAuth flow after cancellation during setup 41ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > waits for Node OAuth runtime before creating an authorization flow 5ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > builds callback redirect URIs from the configured loopback host 1ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > rejects non-loopback callback bind hosts 1ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > times out token exchange requests 5ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > cancels token exchange requests with the caller signal 1ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > rejects unsafe token exchange lifetimes 16ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > times out token refresh requests 1ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > rejects non-positive token refresh lifetimes 1ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > rejects oversized token exchange responses 37ms
 ✓ OpenAI Codex OAuth flow > rejects oversized token refresh responses 36ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  12 passed (12)
```

The oversized tests create 18 MiB ReadableStream responses (cap is 16 MiB) and verify the bounded reader in `postTokenForm` cancels the stream and the error propagates through `exchangeAuthorizationCode` / `refreshAccessToken` as a formatted token failure.

## Risk

Low. The 16 MiB cap matches the codebase convention. The cap is applied at the first body read (inside `postTokenForm`), and downstream bounded readers provide defense-in-depth on the re-wrapped response.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): accept title wikilink targets
target.body:
## Summary
- Accept normalized memory-wiki lint targets for page titles and aliases, not only generated paths and basenames.
- Normalize lint link checks for markdown extension, leading `./`, query strings, and heading fragments so Obsidian title links do not report false `broken-wikilink` warnings.
- Add a memory-wiki lint regression test covering title, title-heading, alias, and relative markdown targets.

## Why
Paperclip SUP-1603 found that `openclaw wiki lint` reported broken wikilinks for bridged Obsidian notes even when matching memory-wiki pages existed. The linter only accepted generated slug paths and basenames, while bridge-generated pages preserve the original note title in frontmatter.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw wiki lint` produced false `broken-wikilink` warnings for bridged Obsidian source notes whose links targeted the human-readable page title instead of the generated slug filename.
- **Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw memory-wiki vault at `/Users/andrew/.openclaw/wiki/main` on macOS, using this patched source branch against the current vault contents.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran this patched-branch command from the OpenClaw source checkout:

```sh
node --import tsx -e 'import { lintMemoryWikiVault } from "./extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.ts"; import { resolveMemoryWikiConfig } from "./extensions/memory-wiki/src/config.ts"; const result = await lintMemoryWikiVault(resolveMemoryWikiConfig({ vaultMode: "bridge", vault: { path: "/Users/andrew/.openclaw/wiki/main", renderMode: "obsidian" }, bridge: { enabled: true } })); const broken = result.issues.filter((issue) => issue.code === "broken-wikilink"); console.log(JSON.stringify({ vaultRoot: result.vaultRoot, issueCount: result.issueCount, brokenWikilinks: broken.length, brokenTargets: broken.map((issue) => issue.message), issuesByCode: result.issues.reduce((acc, issue) => { acc[issue.code] = (acc[issue.code] || 0) + 1; return acc; }, {}), reportPath: result.reportPath }, null, 2));'
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real vault after applying this patch:

```json
{
  "vaultRoot": "/Users/andrew/.openclaw/wiki/main",
  "issueCount": 3,
  "brokenWikilinks": 0,
  "brokenTargets": [],
  "issuesByCode": {
    "missing-id": 1,
    "missing-page-type": 1,
    "stale-page": 1
  },
  "reportPath": "/Users/andrew/.openclaw/wiki/main/reports/lint.md"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real vault no longer reports any `broken-wikilink` issues. Before this patch, the same real vault reported four title-link `broken-wikilink` warnings for `sources/super-ai-coach-video-script-skill-implementation-plan.md`.
- **What was not tested:** A released npm install containing this patch was not tested because this PR has not been merged or released yet.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`

target.title: fix(compaction): make overflow tuning configurable
target.body:
## Summary

- add `agents.defaults.compaction.preemptiveOverflowRatio` for Pi tool-loop preemptive overflow threshold tuning
- add `agents.defaults.compaction.maxOverflowAttempts` for Pi overflow compaction retry tuning
- preserve current defaults (`0.9` ratio, `3` overflow retries) and document schema labels/help text

Fixes #80638
Fixes #80639

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Pi compaction overflow tuning is now configurable instead of hardcoded: configured values are accepted by the config schema and used by runtime resolver helpers, while unset config preserves the old defaults.
- **Real environment tested:** Harry's Mac mini, Darwin 25.4.0 arm64, Node v22.22.0, local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/80638-80639-compaction-knobs`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx - <<'EOF'
import { resolveMaxOverflowCompactionAttempts, resolvePreemptiveOverflowRatio } from './src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-config.ts';
import { AgentDefaultsSchema } from './src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts';

const config = { agents: { defaults: { compaction: { preemptiveOverflowRatio: 0.72, maxOverflowAttempts: 1 } } } };
const parsed = AgentDefaultsSchema.parse(config.agents.defaults);
console.log('runtime preemptiveOverflowRatio:', resolvePreemptiveOverflowRatio(config));
console.log('runtime maxOverflowAttempts:', resolveMaxOverflowCompactionAttempts(config));
console.log('schema preserved preemptiveOverflowRatio:', parsed.compaction?.preemptiveOverflowRatio);
console.log('schema preserved maxOverflowAttempts:', parsed.compaction?.maxOverflowAttempts);
console.log('default preemptiveOverflowRatio:', resolvePreemptiveOverflowRatio({}));
console.log('default maxOverflowAttempts:', resolveMaxOverflowCompactionAttempts({}));
EOF
```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the real local checkout:

```text
runtime preemptiveOverflowRatio: 0.72
runtime maxOverflowAttempts: 1
schema preserved preemptiveOverflowRatio: 0.72
schema preserved maxOverflowAttempts: 1
default preemptiveOverflowRatio: 0.9
default maxOverflowAttempts: 3
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Configured values (`0.72`, `1`) are parsed and resolved at runtime; default behavior remains `0.9` and `3` when unset.
- **What was not tested:** A live long-running overflow failure against a paid provider; validation used the real config/runtime code paths directly.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-config.test.ts src/config/config.compaction-settings.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `pnpm -s config:schema:check`
- `pnpm -s check:test-types`

target.title: feat(voice-call): implement asynchronous email delivery for consult results
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** During realtime voice calls, complex questions (e.g., data queries requiring tool execution) block the caller waiting for an answer that may take minutes, with no fallback if the consult times out or the caller prefers email delivery.
- **Why it matters:** Callers hang up or get silence during long-running agent consults. There's no mechanism to acknowledge the request and deliver results asynchronously.
- **What changed:** Added background email delivery for voice-call consults with two paths: (1) **email-first** — caller explicitly requests email delivery, agent acks immediately and researches in background; (2) **timeout** — if consult exceeds `backgroundConsultTimeoutMs`, ack the caller and email the result when ready. New config fields: `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled` (explicit toggle, default `false`), `backgroundConsultTimeoutMs`, `backgroundEmailPrompt`, `recipientEmail`. New file `background-email-delivery.ts` handles fire-and-forget email delivery via embedded agent runs. Added `deliveryPreference` arg to the consult tool schema. Improved system prompts to prevent narration in background runs and prevent re-querying in email delivery. Email delivery agent spawns are restricted to `["exec", "memory_search", "memory_get"]` tools only. All logs redacted to structural IDs (no args/transcript/result text). Timeout timer properly cleaned up after `Promise.race`.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Normal voice consult path is untouched. No changes to the realtime voice provider, webhook server, call manager, or telephony providers. Core `consultRealtimeVoiceAgent` only gained an optional `abortSignal` param — no behavioral change. Plugin SDK barrel export additions only.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related # (none — feature request from internal testing)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Voice call consults that exceed timeout now ack the caller and deliver results by email. Callers can also explicitly request email delivery. Feature is off by default and requires explicit opt-in via `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled: true`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw dev with Twilio realtime voice, Claude (Anthropic) provider
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Configured `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled: true`, `backgroundConsultTimeoutMs: 15000` and `recipientEmail`, called the voice number, asked a complex data query, observed timeout path trigger and email delivery spawn
- **Evidence after fix:** Logs show `Timeout path: 15000ms exceeded`, `Email-first path: returning ack`, `Spawning email delivery agent`, `Background email delivery completed`
- **Observed result after fix:** Caller receives spoken ack ("I'll research that and send you an email"), background consult completes, email delivery agent spawns and sends email
- **What was not tested:** Tests not yet updated after iterative changes; `pnpm check:changed` not yet run
- **Before evidence:** Before this change, long consults blocked the caller indefinitely or timed out with an error

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — new feature

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.background-email.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/background-email-delivery.test.ts`, `src/realtime-voice/agent-consult-tool.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** email-first path returns ack and spawns delivery; timeout path races and falls back to email; `parseRealtimeVoiceAgentConsultArgs` normalizes `deliveryPreference`; `buildRealtimeVoiceAgentConsultEmailAckResponse` returns correct ack text; both paths gated by `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled`; disabled toggle falls through to normal consult
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Unit tests on the tool schema/args + integration test on the runtime handler paths cover all branching logic
- **If no new test is added, why not:** Test files exist but need updating after iterative changes

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config fields under `plugins.entries.voice-call.config.realtime`:
  - `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled` (boolean, default `false`) — master toggle for the entire background email delivery feature; must be `true` to enable email-first and timeout paths
  - `backgroundConsultTimeoutMs` (number, optional) — timeout before falling back to email (requires `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled`)
  - `backgroundEmailPrompt` (string, optional) — custom system prompt for email delivery agent
  - `recipientEmail` (string, email, optional) — pre-configured recipient; voice agent won't ask for email
- New `deliveryPreference` argument in the realtime voice consult tool (`"voice"` default, `"email"` for explicit email request) — only honored when `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled` is `true`
- Voice agent instructions updated to mention email delivery option
- Graceful shutdown now flushes pending background deliveries

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[caller asks question] -> [consult agent] -> [wait...] -> [timeout/error OR answer]

After (email-first, backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled: true):
[caller asks "email me the answer"] -> [ack immediately] -> [background consult] -> [email delivery agent] -> [email sent]

After (timeout, backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled: true):
[caller asks question] -> [race: consult vs timeout]
  -> consult wins: [return answer to caller]
  -> timeout wins: [ack caller] -> [consult continues] -> [email delivery agent] -> [email sent]

After (backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled: false or unset):
[caller asks question] -> [consult agent] -> [return answer to caller]  (same as before)
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` — email delivery uses existing exec tool (himalaya/gog CLI)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Restricted` — email delivery agent spawns are limited to `["exec", "memory_search", "memory_get"]` tools via `toolsAllow`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- PII in logs? `No` — all tool handler and email delivery logs redacted to structural IDs only (no args, transcript, or result text)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: Anthropic Claude (sonnet)
- Integration/channel: Twilio voice-call realtime
- Relevant config (redacted): `realtime.enabled: true`, `realtime.backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled: true`, `realtime.backgroundConsultTimeoutMs: 15000`, `realtime.recipientEmail: <redacted>`

### Steps

1. Configure voice-call plugin with `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled: true`, `backgroundConsultTimeoutMs`, and `recipientEmail`
2. Call the configured phone number
3. Ask a complex question that requires tool execution (e.g., "What are January purchases by location?")
4. Observe: caller gets ack after timeout, email arrives with full answer

### Expected

- Caller receives spoken acknowledgment after timeout
- Background consult completes with full data
- Email delivery agent sends the answer

### Actual

- Confirmed via live testing with Twilio + Anthropic

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets (runtime logs showing all three paths)
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
```text
14:34:56 [voice-call] realtime tool call executing callId=4b560e0d-15b9-444f-85fc-2f09090c59fd tool=openclaw_agent_consult hasHandler=true
14:34:56 [plugins] [voice-call] Tool handler invoked: tool=openclaw_agent_consult, call=4b560e0d-15b9-444f-85fc-2f09090c59fd, args={"question":"***","context":"User asked: \"Can you please give me a ***\"","responseStyle":"warm, friendly, professional","deliveryPreference":"voice"}, partialTranscript=null
14:34:56 [plugins] [voice-call] Timeout path: racing consult against 15000ms for call=4b560e0d-15b9-444f-85fc-2f09090c59fd, agent=main, session=voice:573042634248
14:35:11 [plugins] [voice-call] Timeout path: 15000ms exceeded for call=4b560e0d-15b9-444f-85fc-2f09090c59fd, deferring to email delivery
14:35:11 [voice-call] realtime tool call completed callId=4b560e0d-15b9-444f-85fc-2f09090c59fd tool=openclaw_agent_consult status=ok elapsedMs=15009
14:35:43 [plugins] [voice-call] Timeout path: background consult resolved for call=4b560e0d-15b9-444f-85fc-2f09090c59fd, result="You have ***"
14:35:43 [plugins] [voice-call] Spawning email delivery agent for session=voice:573042634248, agent=main, question="Provide a summary of ***", consultResult="You have ***"
```

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** email-first path, timeout path, normal path (unaffected), graceful shutdown flush, feature disabled by default (no behavior change without opt-in)
- **Edge cases checked:** missing recipientEmail (falls back to memory lookup), no backgroundConsultTimeoutMs (skips timeout path), empty consult result (fallbackText), `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled: false` (email-first and timeout paths skipped, normal consult only), `deliveryPreference: "email"` ignored when feature disabled
- **What you did not verify:** `pnpm check:changed`, test suite after iterative changes, email actually received (email CLI tool availability on test machine)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — all new config fields are optional; `backgroundEmailDeliveryEnabled` defaults to `false`, so no behavior change without explicit opt-in
- Config/env changes? `Yes` — four new optional config fields under `realtime`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Background consult promise leaks if process crashes before email delivery
  - **Mitigation:** `pendingBackgroundDeliveries` tracked and flushed on graceful shutdown; `.finally()` cleanup on each promise
- **Risk:** Email delivery agent re-queries data instead of using provided answer
  - **Mitigation:** System prompt explicitly forbids re-querying; answer delimited with clear markers; prompt says "Do NOT call any data or API tools"
- **Risk:** Email delivery agent spawns with excessive tool access
  - **Mitigation:** `toolsAllow` restricted to `["exec", "memory_search", "memory_get"]` only
- **Risk:** Timeout timer leaks if consult resolves first
  - **Mitigation:** `clearTimeout(timeoutHandle)` called after `Promise.race` resolves

target.title: fix(auto-reply/followup): surface billing/quota notice instead of silent drop (#80700)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80700.

When the followup agent's model-fallback chain exhausts on a billing, quota, or rate-limit class rejection from the provider, the catch block in `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts` called `replyOperation.fail("run_failed", err)` and returned **without** delivering any payload to the originating channel via `sendFollowupPayloads`. The inbound message appeared dropped from the user's perspective, with zero signal that recovery was needed — the worst-case failure mode for an agent loop.

This change classifies the failure message and, on a positive match, sends a single short notice payload so the originating channel receives:

> ⚠️ Follow-up reply generation failed (billing/quota/rate-limit). Please retry shortly.

## Approach

Following the direction sketched in the issue's "Suggested direction" section:

1. **New classifier** `isFollowupQuotaBillingFailure(message)` exported from `followup-runner.ts`. Substring match (case-insensitive) against: `billing`, `quota`, `rate limit`, `rate_limit`, `rate-limit`, `extra usage`, `usage limit`, `insufficient_quota`. Empty input returns `false`.
2. **Inside the existing catch block**, after `replyOperation.fail()` and the existing error log, if the classifier matches and `run.sourceReplyDeliveryMode !== "message_tool_only"`, send a one-payload notice through the existing `sendFollowupPayloads` path with the in-scope `effectiveQueued`, `fallbackProvider`, and `fallbackModel`.
3. **Notice-send failures** are caught and logged via `defaultRuntime.error?.(...)` so they cannot mask the original error or throw out of the catch.

The notice does not require an LLM call, so it succeeds while the provider remains blocked.

## Why this shape

- **No model-fallback-layer changes.** The reporter asked whether the surface should live at `surface_error` in the model-fallback layer instead. Keeping the change local to the followup catch block matches the existing `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` suppression pattern already in this file (line 458) and is minimally invasive.
- **`message_tool_only` parity.** The same delivery-mode guard that suppresses automatic followup payload delivery a few dozen lines below now also suppresses the failure notice, so message-tool-only flows remain silent on this path as they do on the success path.
- **Explicit notice failure handling.** Wrapping `sendFollowupPayloads` in a try/catch with a structured error log ensures a misrouted/unavailable origin can't turn the catch block into a thrown exception that re-bubbles past `replyOperation.fail()`.

## Tests

Adds 6 regression tests to `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` in a new `createFollowupRunner quota/billing failure notice (#80700)` suite:

1. Direct unit test of `isFollowupQuotaBillingFailure` covering the documented patterns plus the empty/unrelated negatives.
2. Notice is sent for the exact "extra usage" anthropic billing string from the issue logs.
3. Notice is sent for an `insufficient_quota` error.
4. Notice is sent for a `429 ... rate limit` error.
5. Notice is **not** sent for an unrelated `socket hang up` error (negative case).
6. Notice is suppressed when `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"`.

Full file suite: **36/36 passing** (30 pre-existing + 6 new). Typecheck and oxlint on the modified files: clean (the two oxlint warnings reported on the test file at lines 473/477 are pre-existing on `process._getActiveHandles`/`_getActiveRequests` and were untouched by this PR).

## Out of scope / open questions for maintainers

- Whether rate-limit vs. billing should surface as visually distinct notices (e.g., "retry shortly" vs. "owner attention needed"). Kept to a single message here.
- Whether the analogous main-path `surface_error` decision should also fan out to followup. Left untouched.
- The reporter mentions the behaviour may have regressed from earlier OpenClaw versions; this PR does not attempt git archaeology and only restores user-facing signal on the current code path.

Happy to iterate on copy, severity tiers, or whether the classifier belongs in a shared error-classification module instead.

target.title: SUP-1563 restore channel responsiveness health
target.body:
## Summary
- Surface real busy/active-run/stale activity state in channel status instead of relying only on connected transport state.
- Record Telegram outbound activity with the correct account id and avoid treating bot-authored Discord messages as human inbound activity.
- Return visible Discord/Telegram acknowledgements when a message is queued or steered behind active channel work, so connected transports do not look silent during busy turns.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord/Telegram transports could be connected while Jarvis appeared silent because a human Discord message was accepted behind active channel work. The fix exposes busy/stale channel work state, prevents false Discord inbound timestamps from bot-authored gateway events, and keeps Telegram accounts connected while Discord proof is checked against the latest human message.
- Real environment tested: Andrew's Mac mini OpenClaw install, gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`, installed OpenClaw 2026.5.3 runtime plus the SUP-1563 live hotfix, Discord bot account `default`, Telegram accounts `chad`, `default`, `michael`, and `steve`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/openclaw-discord-proof-check.js --json --allow-missing`
  - `curl -fsS --max-time 8 http://127.0.0.1:18789/readyz`
  - `sqlite3 -header -column /Users/andrew/.openclaw/tasks/runs.sqlite "select task_id, source_id, owner_key, runtime, status, datetime(created_at/1000,'unixepoch','localtime') as created, datetime(last_event_at/1000,'unixepoch','localtime') as last_event, substr(error,1,120) as error from task_runs where status in ('running','queued') order by created_at desc limit 20;"`
- Evidence after fix: live terminal output from the verifier showed `completionReady: true`, `gatewayReady: true`, `discordConnected: true`, `discordTurnNotStale: true`, `telegramAllConnected: true`, and `latestDiscordHumanPingHasLaterJarvisReplyOrAck: true`. The latest human Discord message `1503542519265361981` at `2026-05-11T23:41:11.062Z` had later Jarvis reply `1503543303164006632` at `2026-05-11T23:44:17.958Z`. Channel status reported Discord `turn.active: false`, `turn.event: "completed"`, and all four Telegram accounts connected/running. Direct `/readyz` after the proof pass returned `ready:true`, `failing:[]`, and `eventLoop.degraded:false`.
- Observed result after fix: Telegram remained operational, Discord answered the newest observed human message, there were no queued/running task ledger rows, and a follow-up `/readyz` check stayed clean after the heavier channel-status probe.
- What was not tested: No new outbound test messages were sent by the operator; live proof used Andrew's real Discord messages plus read-only Discord REST/status verification.

## Verification
- `CI=true pnpm vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/channels.adds-non-default-telegram-account.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`
- `CI=true NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: [codex] Add manifest plugin auth requirements
target.body:
## Summary

- Add optional native plugin manifest `authRequirements` metadata for provider credentials, channel accounts, external services, and host runtime capabilities.
- Carry the metadata through manifest registry records and export `collectPluginAuthRequirements()` from the plugin registry barrel so benches/setup/install surfaces can consume explicit requirements plus derived hints from existing `setup.providers`, `providerAuthChoices`, provider env vars, and channel env vars.
- Preserve legacy `providerAuthEnvVars` hints by merging them into the matching derived `setup.providers` requirement instead of dropping them when both metadata surfaces mention the same provider.
- Document the contract and ClawHub install implication, with a changelog entry.

## Compatibility

This is additive and metadata-only. Existing native plugins keep loading because `authRequirements` is optional, malformed entries are skipped by the normalizer, compatible bundle manifests are unchanged, and runtime credential checks still stay plugin-owned.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: plugin install/setup/test planners can read a manifest-only auth plan, including a provider declared through both `setup.providers` and legacy `providerAuthEnvVars`, without executing plugin runtime code.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Node via the repo toolchain, using a temporary native plugin manifest loaded by the real manifest loader.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx` from the repo root to write a temporary `openclaw.plugin.json`, load it with `loadPluginManifest(...)`, and call `collectPluginAuthRequirements(...)`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from that real Node command:

```json
[
  {
    "source": "setup-provider",
    "id": "provider:demo",
    "envVars": [
      "DEMO_API_KEY",
      "DEMO_LEGACY_API_KEY"
    ],
    "setupRefs": [
      "setup.providers:demo",
      "providerAuthEnvVars:demo"
    ]
  },
  {
    "source": "provider-env-vars",
    "id": "provider-env:legacy",
    "envVars": [
      "LEGACY_API_KEY"
    ],
    "setupRefs": [
      "providerAuthEnvVars:legacy"
    ]
  },
  {
    "source": "channel-env-vars",
    "id": "channel:slack",
    "envVars": [
      "SLACK_BOT_TOKEN"
    ],
    "setupRefs": [
      "channelEnvVars:slack"
    ]
  }
]
```

- Observed result after fix: the derived `provider:demo` requirement keeps the new setup descriptor, carries both the new and legacy env-var hints, and records both setup references; the unmatched legacy provider and channel hints remain separate plan items.
- What was not tested: no real secret lookup or credential validation, because this PR is intentionally metadata-only and runtime auth remains plugin-owned.

## Verification

- `node --import tsx` real manifest-loader/auth-plan proof above
- `pnpm test src/plugins/auth-requirements.test.ts src/plugins/manifest.json5-tolerance.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

target.title: fix(tools): let apply_patch inherit trusted exec mode
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `apply_patch` stayed workspace-contained even when `exec` was explicitly configured as `security=full` and `ask=off`, forcing trusted owner/operator setups to manage a second, protected `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=false` knob.
- Why it matters: no-approval full exec can already write anywhere the selected host permits, so making `apply_patch` stricter in that mode is inconsistent friction rather than a meaningful security boundary.
- What changed: when `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly` is unset, `apply_patch` now inherits configured no-approval full-exec host-write authority; explicit `applyPatch.workspaceOnly=true` and `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` still force workspace containment.
- What did NOT change: `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=false` remains an explicit dangerous config flag, and approval/allowlist exec modes still keep `apply_patch` workspace-contained by default.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #77886
- Related #62006
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `apply_patch` outside the workspace should work when the operator has already configured trusted no-approval full exec (`tools.exec.security=full`, `tools.exec.ask=off`) and has not explicitly forced `applyPatch.workspaceOnly=true` or `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, Node 24, branch `seb/apply-patch-inherits-full-exec`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```sh
  PATH="/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Cellar/node@24/24.15.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec tsx - <<'TS'
  import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
  import os from 'node:os';
  import path from 'node:path';
  import { createOpenClawCodingTools } from './src/agents/pi-tools.ts';

  const workspace = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'openclaw-real-applypatch-'));
  const escaped = path.join(path.dirname(workspace), `openclaw-real-applypatch-${process.pid}.txt`);
  const rel = path.relative(workspace, escaped);
  try {
    const tools = createOpenClawCodingTools({
      workspaceDir: workspace,
      agentDir: workspace,
      config: { tools: { allow: ['read', 'write', 'exec'], exec: { security: 'full', ask: 'off' } } },
      modelProvider: 'openai',
      modelId: 'gpt-5.5',
    });
    const tool = tools.find((t) => t.name === 'apply_patch');
    if (!tool) throw new Error('apply_patch missing');
    await (tool as any).execute('manual-check', {
      input: `*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: ${rel}\n+ok\n*** End Patch`,
    });
    const content = await fs.readFile(escaped, 'utf8');
    console.log(JSON.stringify({ workspace, escapedExists: content === 'ok\n', content }));
  } finally {
    await fs.rm(escaped, { force: true });
    await fs.rm(workspace, { recursive: true, force: true });
  }
  TS
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```json
  {"workspace":"/tmp/openclaw-real-applypatch-WfVLht","escapedExists":true,"content":"ok\n"}
  ```
- Observed result after fix: the real `apply_patch` tool instance wrote the file outside the temporary workspace under configured `security=full` + `ask=off`.
- What was not tested: a full live Gateway restart/session using this source checkout; the live installed Gateway was not modified.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing regression coverage documents the old behavior in `pi-tools-agent-config.test.ts`: absent an explicit `workspaceOnly=false`, outside-workspace `apply_patch` rejected with `Path escapes sandbox root`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `createOpenClawCodingTools()` computed `applyPatchWorkspaceOnly` solely from `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` and `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly`, ignoring the effective configured exec trust mode.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered explicit `applyPatch.workspaceOnly=false`, but not the coherent trusted-exec case (`security=full`, `ask=off`).
- Contributing context (if known): `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=false` is correctly treated as dangerous/protected for agent-initiated config mutation, which made the duplicate knob especially awkward for owner-operated trusted deployments.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: full exec with approvals off allows outside-workspace `apply_patch` when `applyPatch.workspaceOnly` is unset; full exec with approvals on still blocks; explicit `applyPatch.workspaceOnly=true` and `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` still block.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the actual tool construction path and `apply_patch` execution without requiring a Gateway process.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for trusted exec inheritance; existing explicit opt-out test remains.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- In configured `tools.exec.security=full` + `tools.exec.ask=off` mode, `apply_patch` can write outside the workspace without also setting `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=false`.
- Operators can still force workspace containment with `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=true` or the umbrella `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
exec full/off -> shell can write outside workspace
exec full/off -> apply_patch still blocked unless applyPatch.workspaceOnly=false

After:
exec full/off + applyPatch.workspaceOnly unset -> apply_patch inherits host-write authority
exec full/off + applyPatch.workspaceOnly=true or tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true -> still workspace-contained
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This intentionally makes `apply_patch` match an already trusted/no-approval full-exec configuration. In that mode, `exec` can already write arbitrary files using shell commands. Mitigations: approval/allowlist exec modes still keep `apply_patch` workspace-contained by default; explicit `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=true` and `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` override inheritance and force workspace containment.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: direct local source checkout, Node 24
- Model/provider: OpenAI provider/tool registration path (`modelProvider: openai`, `modelId: gpt-5.5`)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `{ "tools": { "allow": ["read", "write", "exec"], "exec": { "security": "full", "ask": "off" } } }`

### Steps

1. Create a temporary workspace.
2. Construct OpenClaw coding tools with `tools.exec.security=full` and `tools.exec.ask=off`.
3. Invoke `apply_patch` to add a file outside the workspace via a relative `..` path.

### Expected

- `apply_patch` writes the file because no-approval full exec already grants equivalent host-write authority.

### Actual

- The file was created outside the workspace and read back as `ok\n`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted checks run:

```sh
pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.test.ts src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-gateway-tool.test.ts src/security/dangerous-config-flags.test.ts
# Test Files 7 passed (7); Tests 140 passed (140)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.test.ts src/config/types.tools.ts src/config/schema.help.ts docs/tools/apply-patch.md docs/tools/exec.md
# All matched files use the correct format.

pnpm check:changed
# exited 0
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - trusted `security=full` + `ask=off` inherits host-write authority for `apply_patch` when unset.
  - `security=full` + `ask=on-miss` remains workspace-contained by default.
  - `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` still forces workspace containment.
  - explicit `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=true` still forces workspace containment.
- Edge cases checked: existing explicit `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=false` behavior still works; gateway protected-config tests still pass unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live installed Gateway behavior after replacing the installed OpenClaw package with this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes, for approval/allowlist/default workspace-contained modes; intentional behavior change for configured no-approval full exec.
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: deployments with `security=full` + `ask=off` that expected `apply_patch` to remain workspace-only when unset will see broader `apply_patch` writes.
  - Mitigation: set `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=true` or `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`; docs and schema help now call this out.

target.title: fix #95295: [Bug]: Web UI sidebar hides existing child-spawned subagent sessions across agent scopes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The Web UI sidebar Recent Sessions list could hide existing child-spawned subagent transcripts when the current chat scope was another agent, so users could not intentionally rediscover those sessions from the left sidebar.
- Solution: Keep the existing scoped/default behavior, but add an explicit sidebar **All agents** mode for sidebar-owned refreshes only. Checkpoint entry and Sessions-tab mutation refreshes stay scoped to the session's agent.
- What changed: Added sidebar state and UI for the scope toggle, made sidebar-owned requests omit scoped `agentId`, kept cron/global/unknown/archived rows excluded, and added follow-up regressions so All agents cannot leak into checkpoint opener, checkpoint branch/restore, post-send, terminal-final, or deferred session-message refreshes.
- What did NOT change / out of scope: This does not change the chat picker, default sidebar scoping, shared `loadSessions` explicit `agentId` semantics, gateway protocol schema, stored transcript format, migration behavior, public config, or default visibility of subagent/spawned sessions. Making All agents a global Sessions-tab mode is intentionally out of scope.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users with persisted child-spawned subagent work can intentionally find and reopen those existing transcripts from the sidebar without losing the safer scoped default view, and checkpoint/Sessions-tab actions no longer inherit that wider sidebar-only visibility.
- Fixes #95295

## What Problem This Solves

The sidebar needed an intentional way to rediscover persisted child-spawned subagent sessions across agent scopes, but earlier passes made the sidebar All agents state too easy to reuse outside the sidebar boundary. In particular, opening checkpoint recovery from chat, checkpoint branch/restore actions, post-send reloads, terminal-final reloads, and deferred `session.message` reloads could refresh shared `sessionsResult` without an agent scope after All agents was enabled. This update keeps the opt-in sidebar behavior while deriving non-sidebar refresh scope from the current/checkpoint/target session key itself, so Sessions-tab, checkpoint, chat-send, terminal, and deferred message refreshes remain scoped.

## Evidence

- Focused checkpoint opener, branch/restore, post-send, terminal-final, and deferred session-message regressions, requested UI test set, UI CI-failure regression subset, core/core-test TypeScript checks, test-type checks, oxlint, and whitespace checks all passed locally after the follow-up scope repair and CI mock/import repair.
- Existing UI/session-store proof from the prior revision still applies for the sidebar All agents behavior: scoped main results omit the orchestrator child, unscoped All agents results include the child-spawned row with canonical `spawnedBy`, and Chrome showed the subagent row only after pressing **All agents**.
- Proof gap: I still cannot run a long-lived live gateway/Mantis proof in this contributor environment. The local proof uses focused repository tests plus the existing deterministic Control UI/session-store browser proof; it does not claim real Mantis or private live-gateway coverage.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Sidebar Recent Sessions can opt in to cross-agent discovery so an existing row such as `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` with `spawnedBy` can appear when the user enables **All agents**, while default scoped mode still hides those rows. The follow-up repairs also address the review-found scope leaks by keeping the chat checkpoint opener, checkpoint branch/restore, post-send, terminal-final, and deferred session-message `sessions.list` refreshes scoped to the current/checkpoint/target session's agent even when sidebar All agents is enabled.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw repository worktree on Windows/Git Bash with Node `v22.17.0`, pnpm-installed workspace dependencies, and installed Chrome driven by Playwright for the earlier deterministic Control UI proof. The repo engine warning requested Node `>=22.19.0`, but the focused Vitest/lint/diff checks and earlier browser/session-store proof completed successfully.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts -t "keeps opening checkpoints scoped"`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts -t "keeps checkpoint branch and restore refreshes scoped"`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core`; `pnpm tsgo:core:test`; `pnpm check:test-types`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts`; `git diff --check`. Earlier proof also ran the Control UI browser flow and a persisted-store `sessions.list` gateway-handler proof for the sidebar All agents behavior.
- **Evidence after fix:** The focused checkpoint opener regression exited 0 and asserted the chat checkpoint action refreshes `sessions.list` with `agentId: "review"` even when `sidebarRecentSessionsAllAgents` is true. The focused checkpoint mutation regression exited 0 and asserted branch/restore for `agent:review:chat` refresh `sessions.list` with `agentId: "review"` under the same All agents state. The new post-send, terminal-final, and deferred session-message regressions exited 0 and assert those shared refreshes still pass `agentId: "ops"` while the sidebar All agents toggle is enabled. The CI-failure regression subset exited 0 after adding the missing app-render import and app-chat mock exports used by full UI CI lanes. `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, and `pnpm check:test-types` exited 0 after the import and scope repairs. The requested UI command exited 0 and includes the checkpoint, shared-refresh, sidebar renderer, and helper coverage. Oxlint on the requested app/session files exited 0. `git diff --check` exited 0. The earlier stronger session-store/browser proof showed scoped main results omit the orchestrator child, unscoped all-agents results include the child row with canonical `spawnedBy`, and Chrome shows `Subagent: Planner` only after **All agents** is pressed.
- **Observed result after fix:** Sidebar-owned All agents refreshes remain unscoped only for the sidebar Recent Sessions list, while the chat checkpoint opener derives list scope from the current session key, checkpoint branch/restore refreshes derive their list scope from the checkpoint session key (`agent:review:*` -> `agentId: "review"`) or selected global agent, and post-send/terminal/deferred message refreshes derive scope from the active refresh target/session (`agent:ops:*` -> `agentId: "ops"`). This preserves the default scoped view and prevents the sidebar toggle from widening Sessions-tab/checkpoint/chat shared refreshes.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a long-lived live gateway or real Mantis/private transcript proof in this environment. I also did not expand testing into unrelated protocol/build/typecheck matrices beyond the requested focused UI command, the new checkpoint regression, oxlint on changed session controller files, and `git diff --check`.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 (`status: waiting on author` label):** Addressed by this update; the label can clear after bot/maintainer re-review.
- **RF-002 (restore/replace scoped session helper import):** Fixed. Restored the missing `scopedAgentListParamsForSession` import in `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` pass locally.
- **RF-003 (checkpoint entry regression with All agents enabled):** Fixed. Added `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts` coverage for opening checkpoints from chat while `sidebarRecentSessionsAllAgents` is true, plus `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts` coverage for checkpoint branch and restore under the same toggle state.
- **RF-004 (TypeScript failure from removed helper import):** Fixed by the import repair; the core and core-test TypeScript checks pass locally after the fix.
- **RF-005 (P1 checkpoint/Sessions-tab scope leak):** Fixed. The chat checkpoint opener now calls scoped current-session list params, and checkpoint branch/restore refresh calls a session-key-derived list-scope helper, so `agent:review:*` checkpoint actions refresh with `agentId: "review"` instead of using an unscoped/sidebar-wide list request.
- **RF-006 (sibling candidate PRs / canonical product choice):** Disclosed. PRs #95304 and #95307 remain sibling candidates; this PR intentionally keeps the repair UI-only and same-contract. Final product/canonical selection remains a maintainer decision.
- **RF-007 (restore scoped session helper import at `app-render.helpers.ts`):** Fixed by restoring the import and validating TypeScript.
- **RF-008 (keep checkpoint entry loads scoped outside the sidebar):** Fixed. Opening checkpoint recovery from chat no longer uses the sidebar All agents helper; it refreshes sessions with scoped current-session params. Checkpoint mutation refresh scope likewise no longer reads or inherits the sidebar toggle; it derives from the checkpoint session key or selected global agent.
- **RF-009 (`pnpm tsgo:core`):** Completed locally with exit 0.
- **RF-010 (`pnpm check:test-types`):** Completed locally with exit 0.
- **RF-011 (requested focused Vitest command):** Completed locally with exit 0: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`.
- **RF-012 (requested oxlint command):** Completed locally with exit 0: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts`.
- **RF-013 (`git diff --check`):** Completed locally with exit 0.
- **RF-014 (keep post-send refresh scoped):** Fixed. Successful post-send refreshes now use `scopedAgentListParamsForRefreshTarget`, so enabling sidebar All agents does not remove the run target `agentId` from shared `sessionsResult` reloads.
- **RF-015 (keep terminal-final refresh scoped):** Fixed. Terminal final refreshes now use `scopedAgentListParamsForRefreshTarget`, preserving the queued run target's agent scope even when the sidebar toggle is enabled.
- **RF-016 (keep deferred session-message refresh scoped):** Fixed. Deferred `session.message` refreshes now use `scopedAgentListParamsForSession`, preserving the active session's agent scope before replaying the deferred history reload.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`, and `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`, with neighboring focused suite `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` included in the requested command.
- **Target test files:** `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`, and `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`, with `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` included in the requested command.
- **Scenario locked in:** Explicit non-sidebar `loadSessions(..., { agentId })` requests keep their scoped `agentId`; the sidebar All agents control is reachable with an empty recent list; a cross-agent `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` row with `spawnedBy` renders in Recent Sessions all-agents mode while cron rows remain filtered; opening checkpoint recovery from chat stays scoped to the current session's agent; checkpoint branch/restore refreshes stay scoped to the checkpoint session's agent; and post-send, terminal-final, and deferred session-message refreshes stay scoped to their active target/session even when the sidebar All agents toggle is active.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The controller, chat, and gateway tests guard the request boundary so checkpoint/Sessions-tab/chat shared refreshes cannot be accidentally widened, the helper/render tests guard sidebar-owned All agents behavior, and no gateway/protocol/storage contract is expanded.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state / security-boundary / compatibility.
- **Risk explanation:** The diff touches session-state UI filtering and a scope control, which can affect whether internal child-spawned transcripts are visible. Compatibility risk is intentionally limited because explicit `loadSessions` agent overrides remain authoritative and no public protocol, schema, config, storage format, or migration contract changes. Related sibling PRs #95304 and #95307 remain broader/candidate approaches; this PR deliberately stays on a narrower same-contract UI path.
- **Why acceptable:** The new behavior is default-off, explicitly user-selected, and limited to the sidebar Recent Sessions list. Existing exclusions for archived/global/unknown/cron rows remain. Checkpoint and Sessions-tab refreshes now stay scoped, so the All agents state does not widen non-sidebar refreshes.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The original sidebar bug was a request/filter invariant mismatch: Recent Sessions requested current-agent rows and then filtered out subagent/spawned rows, making persisted child-spawned sessions from another agent impossible to rediscover from the sidebar. The follow-up review found a second boundary bug: the sidebar All agents request helper was reused by refreshes that are not sidebar-owned.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** Sidebar-owned refreshes now own the All agents opt-in behavior, while checkpoint opener and mutation refreshes derive their agent scope from the current/checkpoint session key. That keeps the sidebar request/filter invariant correct without changing shared gateway contracts or making All agents a global Sessions-tab mode.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix with a bounded follow-up repair for the review-found scope leak.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the scoped code/test repair; maintainer product selection remains required because sibling PRs exist with different scopes.
- **Patch quality notes:** The `state as unknown as Parameters<typeof loadSessions>[0]` cast remains confined to the existing UI helper boundary where `AppViewState` is structurally passed to `loadSessions`; the follow-up checkpoint repair does not add another type escape. The added `return false` branches in `app-render.ts` are explicit filter-preserving guard branches for cron/global/unknown/archived/default-off subagent rows, not symptom-masking fallbacks. The public-contract warning is addressed by keeping the canonical protocol/schema unchanged and using existing unscoped `sessions.list` only from the explicit sidebar toggle request. The diff is larger than an XS patch because it includes the original UI state/control/render coverage plus this focused checkpoint-scope repair; the review blocker itself is fixed in the small app-render/controller test follow-up.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary remains the existing `sessions.list` contract and combined session store. Sidebar Recent Sessions owns the **All agents** opt-in request/filter behavior; checkpoint/Sessions-tab mutation refreshes own their selected-session scope and derive `agentId` from the session key. This patch does not add schema, protocol, public config, data-format, storage, migration, or gateway-contract changes.

target.title: fix(feishu): bound Feishu API JSON response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a shared Feishu JSON response wrapper that delegates to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http` while capping credentialed Feishu API JSON bodies at 16 MiB.
- Routes app-registration JSON reads through the shared helper, covering init, device-code begin, polling, and app-owner lookup.
- Extends the same cap to the sibling streaming-card token and card-create JSON reads, addressing the previous review finding about an uncovered Feishu credentialed path.
- Rebases cleanly onto latest `upstream/main` and keeps the upstream scan-to-create QR terminal rendering test.

## Linked context

No linked issue; this is a bounded-response-read hardening follow-up for Feishu/Lark credentialed API JSON responses.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Feishu app-registration and streaming-card credentialed API paths previously had successful JSON reads that could buffer an unbounded response body.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86-64, local OpenClaw repo environment, real `ReadableStream`/`Response` body paths through production functions after rebasing on 2026-06-29.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules \
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/app-registration.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts -- --run
  PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules \
    /media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint \
    extensions/feishu/src/app-registration.ts extensions/feishu/src/app-registration.test.ts \
    extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts \
    extensions/feishu/src/json-response.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  [feishu fetchFeishuJson bound proof] over-cap: bytes_pulled=17825792 cap=16777216 canceled=true
  [feishu fetchFeishuJson bound proof] under-cap: returned={"deviceCode":"dev-code-123",...}
  [feishu streaming-card bound proof] token over-cap: bytes_pulled=17825855 cap=16777216 canceled=true
  [feishu streaming-card bound proof] card-create over-cap: bytes_pulled=17825855 cap=16777216 canceled=true
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  26 passed (26)
  ```

Normal under-cap app-registration and streaming-card flows still parse and pass existing tests.

- **Before evidence:** Previous branch was conflicting against main and only covered app-registration. The sibling `streaming-card.ts` token reader remained a bare JSON reader before this update.

target.title: fix(sessions): avoid cross-cwd recent resumes
target.body:
Closes #96542

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users resuming a recent session from one project could silently load another project's transcript when the two project paths encode to the same default session directory, such as `/home/alice/dev/client/app` and `/home/alice/dev/client-app`.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix keeps the existing shipped session-directory encoding intact and makes recent-session selection cwd-aware instead. `continueRecent` now only resumes JSONL files whose persisted session header `cwd` matches the requested cwd, and the normal per-project session list uses the same header-cwd filter. `listAll` remains unchanged because it is intentionally cross-project.

This avoids orphaning existing session directories while preventing a colliding directory from adopting the newest transcript for a different project.

## User Impact

Continuing a session from a project now resumes only that project's transcript, or starts fresh when no matching session exists. Users no longer get another project's conversation history mixed into the current cwd when path separators and literal hyphens collide in the encoded session directory.

## Evidence

- Duplicate PR gate:
  - `gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 50 --json number,title,url,updatedAt,body -- "96542 OR \"project cwds differing\" OR \"getDefaultSessionDir\" OR \"client-app\""` -> `[]`
- Real on-disk resume-path proof for the CLI/gateway session-manager boundary:
  - Command: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from this branch, using the production `getDefaultSessionDir`, `findMostRecentSession`, `SessionManager.continueRecent`, and `SessionManager.list` exports against two real JSONL session headers in one isolated temp agent dir.
  - Observed terminal output, redacted only for the temp root:

```text
OpenClaw cwd-collision resume proof
agent dir: <tmp>/agent
cwd A: /home/alice/dev/client/app
cwd B: /home/alice/dev/client-app
default dirs collide: true
session A file: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-00-000Z_session-a.jsonl
session B file: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-01-000Z_session-b.jsonl
unfiltered newest file: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-01-000Z_session-b.jsonl
cwd-filtered newest for cwd A: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-00-000Z_session-a.jsonl
continueRecent(cwd A) file: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-00-000Z_session-a.jsonl
continueRecent(cwd A) header cwd: /home/alice/dev/client/app
continueRecent(cwd A) crossed cwd: false
SessionManager.list(cwd A) files: <tmp>/agent/sessions/--home-alice-dev-client-app--/2026-06-18T00-00-00-000Z_session-a.jsonl
SessionManager.list(cwd A) cwds: /home/alice/dev/client/app
```

  - Claim proved: the two example cwds still collide under the shipped directory encoding, but the fixed real resume selector does not adopt the newer JSONL file from the other cwd; `continueRecent(cwd A)` resumes only the session whose header `cwd` is `cwd A`, and `SessionManager.list(cwd A)` filters out the other cwd.
- Focused regression test:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 48 tests passed.
- Touched-file formatting/lint:
  - `node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.CMD --check src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.
  - `node_modules\.bin\oxlint.CMD src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts` -> exit 0.
- Patch hygiene:
  - `git diff --check` -> clean.
  - `git diff --cached --check` -> clean before commit.
- Autoreview:
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` -> `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Not tested / proof gaps:

- I did not run a provider-backed LLM turn or full gateway RPC end to end. The live terminal proof above drives the production session-manager resume/list boundary that the CLI/gateway resume-most-recent path depends on, with real on-disk JSONL session files and the colliding default session directory.

AI-assisted: yes. No agent transcript is included because explicit transcript approval was not requested during this fast contributor workflow.
